title: bd-timew merge decision: stays separate or folds into claude-config?
description:
Design-pass bead, gating sidecar config (ClaudeConfig-wy9) work.

Decide whether bd-timew (currently lives outside this project) should be merged into the claude-config tree. The decision affects:
  - Where bd-timew CLI tools install from (claude-config's sandbox/scripts vs. its own repo)
  - Who owns the schema for <project>/.beads/bd-timew.yaml
  - The nested-git-repo-for-jsonl-state pattern under <project>/.beads/
  - Whether the sidecar's setup.beads + teardown.beads sections supplement or replace any current bd-timew config surfaces

Gates: the claude-session.yaml beads: schema design (sidecar epic ClaudeConfig-wy9). Can't lock that schema cleanly without knowing the project boundary.

Discussed in 2026-06-01 sidecar design discussion (auto-memory: feedback-bdtimew-merge-question if filed). User raised this as motivation for declarative teardown.beads — claude-session-specific deviations from project's normal beads behavior (timetracking unit, Dolt branch strategy, reconciliation, labels/templates).

Acceptance:
  - DECISION_LOG entry capturing merge-or-not + rationale
  - If merge: migration plan filed as follow-up beads
  - If not: documented scope boundary so sidecar schema can reference bd-timew's existing surfaces without owning them
notes: Scoped out to external bead in the bd-timew project's beads instance per 2026-06-02 design discussion. The claude-config-side decisions captured in DEC-026/027/028 with revision-possible caveats on the beads: section schema. Closing here.

title: Provision claude-session git identity + claude-config-shipped core.hooksPath dir
description:
Foundation 3 of 5 — per ~/.claude/plans/quizzical-nibbling-crab.md.

Two intertwined deliverables:

1. claude-session git identity (provisioning):
   Add a step to sandbox/scripts/provision-claude-session.sh that writes
   claude-session's ~/.gitconfig with:
   - user.name = configurable (default "claude-session")
   - user.email = configurable (default "claude-session@<hostname>")
   - init.defaultBranch = main (or copy from host user's setting)
   - commit.gpgsign = false (deliberate; GPG is opt-in via F-git2)
   - core.hooksPath = /home/claude-session/.config/git/hooks
     (XDG-style; matches the Lua-hook deployment pattern)

2. claude-config-shipped git hooks directory:
   Source: claude/git-hooks/<hook>
   Install: ${SHARE_DIR}/git-hooks/<hook> (via per-domain install recipe
     under sandbox/ — actually under claude/, since the source is there;
     determine the right domain ownership during implementation)
   Mirror: /home/claude-session/.config/git/hooks/ via assemble_claude_dir
     (extend the existing skills/agents/commands/scripts/hooks loop to
     also handle git-hooks)
   Profile: sandbox_home gains ${SANDBOX_HOME}/.config/git/hooks ro bind.

   Initial contents (stub-only; real logic lands in bi0.2's decide()
   extraction or a tightly-coupled follow-up):
   - pre-commit: stub that exits 0 with a stderr trace line "claude-config
     pre-commit (stub); decision logic lands with bi0.2".
   - pre-push: same stub pattern.

Note: project hooks are EXCLUDED by default (core.hooksPath redirects to
claude-config's dir). The sidecar `chain_project_hooks` opt-in (F-git3,
gated on DEC-023) is what re-enables them per-project.

Acceptance:
- `sudo -u claude-session git config --get user.email` returns the
  provisioned value.
- `sudo -u claude-session git config --get core.hooksPath` returns
  /home/claude-session/.config/git/hooks.
- Inside a sandboxed session: `git config core.hooksPath` returns the same.
- Stub hooks present + executable in the in-sandbox path.

Blocks bi0.2 (so the hook dir exists when the protected-branch logic
lands and the git-side pre-commit can be wired to call into decide()).
notes: Closed 2026-06-01: claude/scripts/git-hooks/{pre-commit,pre-push} stub hooks + claude/scripts/git-hooks/justfile (install/uninstall/verify recipes); wrapper SHARE_GIT_HOOKS_DIR resolver + assemble_claude_dir mirror to ~/.config/git/hooks; profile sandbox_home bind; provision-claude-session.sh step 9 sets user.name/user.email/init.defaultBranch/commit.gpgsign=false/core.hooksPath. End-to-end verified — claude-session commit fires pre-commit stub; journald entry present; commit succeeds. Real decision logic lands when bi0.2's decide() is extracted into a callable CLI.

title: Colocate unit tests with domain code; root tests/ for integration tests
description:
Foundation 2.5 of 5 (sibling of F-just1) — per ~/.claude/plans/quizzical-
nibbling-crab.md.

Migrate unit tests next to the code they cover (matching the doc + install
colocation pattern). Root tests/ reserved for true integration tests
(empty until those are written).

Migration:
- tests-lua/_lib_spec.lua          → claude/scripts/hooks/tests/_lib_spec.lua
- tests-lua/config-guard_spec.lua  → claude/scripts/hooks/tests/config-guard_spec.lua
- tests-lua/audit-event_spec.lua   → claude/scripts/hooks/tests/audit-event_spec.lua
- tests-lua/git-guard_spec.lua     → claude/scripts/hooks/tests/git-guard_spec.lua
- tests-bats/test_install_manifest.bats → deleted by F-just1 (manifest retired);
  per-domain bats tests added under sandbox/tests/ as their respective
  install recipes mature.
- tests/test_package_smoke.py      → src/claude_config/tests/test_package_smoke.py
  (or stays at root if no domain owns it; defer)
- tests/                           → reserved for cross-domain integration
  tests (sandbox + hooks + auditing E2E).

Per-domain Justfile `test` recipes point at local tests. Root `just test`
aggregates: `just sandbox::test && just hook::test && just integration::test`.

Acceptance:
- All existing busted (41) + bats (5) + pytest (1) cases pass in their new
  locations.
- Root `just test` aggregates correctly; returns non-zero if any sub-suite
  fails.

Blocks bi0.2 (so its new tests land in the right place from day one).
notes: Closed 2026-06-01: Lua tests moved to claude/scripts/hooks/tests/; root tests/ stays for pytest (smoke) + future cross-domain integration. New test-hooks recipe in hooks justfile; root just test invokes it. luals workspace.ignoreDir excludes test trees from strict checking (busted's monkey-patched assert breaks static analysis; busted itself is the rigor source). docs/architecture/hooks.md updated. Python test stays at root tests/ — single smoke not worth a second restructure.

title: Lua + Bash formatter/linter stack: stylua + shfmt + lua-language-server (strict)
description:
Foundation 1 of 5 — per ~/.claude/plans/quizzical-nibbling-crab.md.

Adopt full polyglot fmt + lint coverage with strict-mode static type checking
for Lua, matching the production-quality-code intent (NOT the user's
personal-nvim baseline which uses light annotations).

Tools to provision (apt for host + claude-session):
- stylua    — Lua formatter (Rust, opinionated)
- shfmt     — Bash formatter (Go, opinionated)
- lua-language-server — `--check` mode for strict diagnostic enforcement

Configuration:
- Project root: stylua.toml (copy from ~/.config/nvim/stylua.toml; 2-space,
  120-col)
- Project root: .luarc.json with strict diagnostics
  (Lua.diagnostics.disable = []; type-checking enabled; un-annotated
  public APIs fail the check)

Justfile recipes:
- `just fmt` — ruff format + shfmt -w + stylua
- `just fmt-check` — all three in --check mode
- `just lua-check` — lua-language-server --check (strict)
- Wire `just lua-check` + `just fmt-check` into the existing pre-commit
  gate (.pre-commit-config.yaml's just-check recipe).

Acceptance:
- `apt list --installed | grep -E 'stylua|shfmt|lua-language-server'` shows
  all three (host).
- `just lua-check` is clean on existing hook code (with F-fmt2 to retrofit
  full annotations afterward).
- pre-commit fires luals --check on Lua edits and fails on type/diagnostic
  errors.

Blocks bi0.2 + every subsequent Lua-touching bead.
notes: Closed 2026-06-01: install-dev-tools.sh (host + claude-session via PREFIX env), stylua.toml (mirroring nvim setup: 2-space, 120-col), .luarc.json (strict-mode diagnostics — all type-mismatch + missing-* classes elevated to Error), just dev-tools + fmt/fmt-check/lua-check recipes wired into just check. Repo-wide stylua + shfmt reformat applied (behavior preserved; 67 busted + 5 bats + 1 pytest still pass). LuaCATS annotations deferred to F-fmt2 per the plan; strict-mode lua-check currently passes on un-annotated code because luals only diagnoses against declared types. Deviation from bead description: apt is empty for all three tools; install via GitHub releases (HTTPS + best-effort sha256).

title: Nested Justfile: per-domain install-<slug> recipes; retire _install-manifest.sh
description:
Foundation 2 of 5 — per ~/.claude/plans/quizzical-nibbling-crab.md.

Restructure the project Justfile into a root + per-domain layout following
the Banfield pattern (~/Source/Banfield/NBU/banfield-marketing-ops/) and
confluence-connector's modular justfiles.

Structure:
- /justfile             — root: orchestrate + cross-cutting recipes
  (`just check`, `just test`, top-level `just install` that delegates).
- /sandbox/justfile     — sandbox-domain recipes: install-bin, install-share,
  install-profile, install-managed-settings, install-etc-sudoers,
  install-git-hooks, smoke, provision, unprovision, verify.
- /claude/scripts/hooks/justfile — hook-domain recipes: install-libs,
  install-scripts (the .lua hooks), install-config (git-guard.yaml).
- Root `just install` declares sub-Justfile install recipes as deps.

Retire:
- sandbox/scripts/_install-manifest.sh — folded into per-domain
  install-<slug> recipes.
- sandbox/scripts/_hooks-manifest.sh — moved into the hooks-domain
  justfile (the .hooks JSON block becomes a recipe; the wrapper still
  reads it but from the new location).

Pattern per domain: each sub-justfile globs (or explicitly lists) its
installable files; applies the right mode via `install -m <mode>`; per-
file mtime-skip if useful. Uses `just`'s recipe-dep syntax to compose
multi-step installs (`install: install-libs install-scripts`).

Acceptance:
- `find . -name justfile` returns at least 3 files (root + 2 domain).
- `just install` end-to-end deploys to /tmp prefix correctly (smoke
  verified).
- Files under _install-manifest.sh's prior scope still land at the same
  install paths.

Blocks F-test1 (test-tree restructure) + bi0.2.
notes: Closed 2026-06-01: nested justfile structure landed. sandbox/justfile + claude/scripts/hooks/justfile imported flat into root. _install-manifest.sh + tests-bats/test_install_manifest.bats retired. install/uninstall/verify at root delegate to per-domain recipes via just's recipe-dep mechanism. install-test PREFIX overrides flow through. just check stays green throughout (just test gracefully skips bats while F-test1 lands per-domain coverage).

title: Scaffold uv-managed Python project (pyproject.toml + dev .venv + entry-point shell)
description:
Per DEC-019: scaffold a uv-managed Python project at the repo root.

Deliverables:
- pyproject.toml at repo root with [project], [project.scripts] (empty
  placeholder for the first real entry point), [dependency-groups.dev]
  (pytest, ruff, mypy at minimum), [tool.ruff], [tool.pytest],
  [tool.mypy].
- src/ layout: src/claude_config/__init__.py (placeholder).
- .python-version (pinned interpreter).
- .gitignore additions: .venv, __pycache__, .ruff_cache, .mypy_cache,
  dist/, build/, *.egg-info/.
- `uv sync` produces a working dev .venv.
- `uv build` produces a wheel (even if empty / minimal).
- README note (or CLAUDE.md addendum) referencing the uv-project shape.

This is the foundation that subsequent Python tools (broker logic,
emitter port if/when, validation utilities) build on. Does not add
deployed Python tools yet — that comes via the corresponding epic 2.5
provisioning bead once at least one entry point is real.

Refs: DEC-019.
notes: Closed 2026-05-29: scaffolded pyproject.toml (uv_build backend per python-projects/confluence-connector reference) + src/claude_config/__init__.py + .python-version (3.12) + uv.lock + .gitignore. uv sync ✓ (13 packages); uv build ✓ (3.2 KB wheel, 3.1 KB sdist); uv run ruff/mypy/pytest ✓. [project.scripts] left commented — populates when Python entry points land. ruff extend-exclude the WIP config-guard.py pending its Lua port (40s.18). Classifiers kept Python-only; Lua/Bash classifiers added if/when Python entry points actually orchestrate those languages.

title: Polyglot dev tooling foundation (uv Python project + Justfile + per-language testing)
description:
Scaffold the project-level dev/build/install tooling per DEC-019,
DEC-021, and DEC-022:

- A uv-managed Python project at the repo root (pyproject.toml,
  [project.scripts] entry points, dev .venv, ruff/pytest/mypy config).
- A Justfile that supersedes the Makefile as the polyglot orchestrator
  (`just build`, `just test`, `just install`, `just check`).
- Per-language native testing: pytest (Python), bats (Bash), busted
  (Lua, when it lands), go test (when Go arrives).
- Provisioning step that pipx/uv-tool-installs the packaged Python
  tools into claude-session's home (DEC-016 model).

Children cover each piece. Implementation can run partly in parallel
(uv scaffold + Justfile port + bats can all start without each other).

Refs: DEC-019, DEC-021, DEC-022.

title: Provision LuaJIT + luarocks + lyaml + lua-cjson for claude-session
description:
Per DEC-017, install in claude-session's home (idempotent):
- LuaJIT (preferred for ~1ms cold start; fallback PUC-Rio Lua 5.4 if
  LuaJIT unavailable on the host distro).
- luarocks (per-Lua-version package manager).
- lyaml (LibYAML binding; YAML parser for fast-path hook configs).
- lua-cjson (JSON I/O for PreToolUse hook stdin/stdout contract).

Pattern follows the Node + srt provisioning step added in 40s.15.11:
heredoc-piped sudo -u claude-session bash -s --; install to
~/.local/{lib,bin}; symlink the lua/luajit binaries onto claude-session's
PATH; idempotent re-runs on version match.

Acceptance:
- `sudo -u claude-session lua -e "require('yaml'); require('cjson'); print('ok')"`
  prints "ok" on a fresh install and on re-run.
- Adds a step to sandbox/scripts/provision-claude-session.sh (parallel
  to step 6 Node/srt install).
- Version-pinned via env vars (LUAJIT_VERSION / LYAML_VERSION /
  LUA_CJSON_VERSION) with sensible defaults; checksum-verified
  downloads where upstream provides checksums.

BLOCKS: ClaudeConfig-bi0 + children (git-guard, language-switched to
Lua per DEC-017), 40s.18 (config-guard), 40s.19 (audit), 40s.21
(telemetry hook).
notes: Closed 2026-05-29: provisioning step 7 added to sandbox/scripts/provision-claude-session.sh. apt-packages path chosen over build-from-source (cleaner, no libyaml-dev/gcc/make host deps); apt-resolved versions tracked in ~/.local/share/claude-session/lua-toolchain.version. Verified idempotent re-run + end-to-end YAML→JSON smoke as claude-session. luajit=2.1.0~beta3+git20220320 lyaml=6.2.8-2 cjson=2.1.0+dfsg-2.2.

title: Provision claude-session interpreter toolchain (Lua + multi-interpreter)
description:
Provision the interpreter toolchain in claude-session's home so that:

1. System-shipped fast-path PreToolUse hooks (config-guard, git-guard,
   audit, telemetry) can run on LuaJIT per DEC-017.
2. Project-specific hooks can be authored in any of Python / Node /
   Bash / Perl / Lua via shebang dispatch per DEC-020.

Python (via uv) and Node + npm + srt are already provisioned by

the smoke-coverage that verifies all five are callable.

Children:
- LuaJIT + luarocks + lyaml + lua-cjson provisioning
- Perl availability verification (system perl; no install needed)
- Multi-interpreter smoke-test extension

Refs: DEC-017, DEC-020.
notes: Closed 2026-05-29: all 3 children done. 58n.1 (LuaJIT + lyaml + lua-cjson + lua symlink), 58n.2 (Perl verify + supported-interpreter docs), 58n.3 (smoke-test.sh multi-interpreter survey). claude-session now has the 5 supported project-hook interpreters callable via shebang dispatch per DEC-020.

title: Survey existing agent-sandbox tooling (kubernetes-sigs/agent-sandbox, agent-infra/sandbox, e2b.dev)
description:
Deep-dive comparative investigation of three open-source agent-sandboxing tools, in light of the claude-config Phase 1+ implementation plan.

## Tools to investigate

- https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/agent-sandbox — Kubernetes-based agent sandboxing
- https://github.com/agent-infra/sandbox — Docker-based agent sandboxing
- https://e2b.dev/ — hosted code-execution sandbox for AI agents

## Methodology

Three Opus general-purpose research agents in parallel, one per package. Each writes a focused report to `.tasks/40s.12-sandbox-tooling-survey/<package-slug>.md` covering:

- Architecture and isolation mechanism (containers / VMs / namespaces / unikernels?)
- Feature set and capabilities (filesystem isolation, network policy, credential model, lifecycle, multi-session, observability)
- Operational model (self-hosted vs hosted; deployment surface; runtime requirements)
- Authentication and credential handling
- Differences from claude-config's plan (DEC-006 allow-list, DEC-007 bwrap, DEC-008 tiered credentials, DEC-009/010, ROADMAP Phases 1–7)
- Adoption/integration feasibility for this user's specific use case (single workstation, WSL2, BOCO context, host-tooling-integrated workflow)

After agent reports return, synthesize a comparative report at `.tasks/40s.12-sandbox-tooling-survey/SUMMARY.md`.

## Final deliverable: SUMMARY.md

Four required sections:

1. **Conclusions on scope-reduction opportunities.** Whether any of these apps represent an opportunity to adopt upstream technology and reduce claude-config implementation scope. Concrete recommendations: which DECs/beads would be re-scoped, what's saved, what's lost.
2. **Follow-up questions raised by the research.**
3. **Related technologies to investigate** (synthesizer's identification, informed by what agents surface): e.g., Firecracker, Kata Containers, sandbox-exec, gVisor, Apple Containerization, sysbox.
4. **One-line value-proposition assessment.** Where claude-config sits in the wider open-source AI-sandboxing landscape, in light of these findings.

## Acceptance

- Three per-package reports persisted at `.tasks/40s.12-sandbox-tooling-survey/`
- SUMMARY.md synthesized with the four required sections
- If scope-reduction opportunities identified, follow-up beads filed and relevant DECs cross-referenced for amendment
- Decision recorded in the bead's closing note: adopt / hybridize / stay course
close_reason: Stay course. bwrap remains Phase 1 primitive (DEC-007 unchanged). All three surveyed tools (kubernetes-sigs/agent-sandbox, agent-infra/sandbox, e2b.dev) address an orthogonal problem: 'spawn isolated runtimes for agent outputs in a SaaS/cluster context.' claude-config addresses 'wrap an interactive Claude Code session with host-integrated tooling on a workstation' — distinct niche, ~zero overlap. One Phase 7 architecture refinement filed as ClaudeConfig-tpn.1: design the executor as a multi-backend dispatcher with E2B and AIO Sandbox as candidate backends for untrusted-code-execution and browser/computer-use capability classes. One Phase 1 follow-up filed as ClaudeConfig-40s.13: investigate Claude Code's own sandboxing primitives. SUMMARY.md and per-tool reports persisted at .tasks/40s.12-sandbox-tooling-survey/.

title: Evaluate bubblewrap as Phase 1 sandbox primitive (allow-list policy)
description:
Evaluate bubblewrap (`bwrap`, github.com/containers/bubblewrap) as the Phase 1 sandbox primitive, replacing the planned `unshare --mount` + `sudo -u claude-session` + shadow-tmpfs approach.

## Why we're considering this

Discovered 2026-05-05 while drafting `sandbox/profiles/default.yaml` for ClaudeConfig-40s.4: a deny-list of credential paths (shadow-tmpfs each sensitive directory) is brittle and tail-risky. New tools install new credential paths constantly (`~/.cargo/credentials.toml`, `~/.git-credentials`, `~/.local/share/keyrings/`, `~/.terraform.d/credentials.tfrc.json`, future-tool-X). A miss in the list is a permanent credential leak.

The fix is a policy flip: shadow-mount the entire `~/` (or a wider scope) by default, then bind-mount BACK only the paths the active profile permits. Allow-list, not deny-list. Same kernel mechanism (mount namespaces); opposite default.

Bubblewrap implements that policy natively:
- Allow-list semantics by default (`--bind <src> <dst>` adds; nothing else visible)
- Single binary, no daemon, no images, no registry
- Doesn't require root (uses user namespaces) — likely obviates the separate `claude-session` user account and sudoers rule entirely
- Battle-tested (Flatpak's sandbox runtime)
- Available in every distro's package manager

## Acceptance for this evaluation

- Build a minimal proof-of-concept `claude-sandbox` wrapper using `bwrap` that runs `claude -p "echo hello"` successfully against the canonical `~/.claude/` config.
- Verify allow-list property: a credential path NOT explicitly bound in (e.g., `~/.password-store/`) is invisible inside the sandbox.
- Verify memory write-back: a `bd remember` from inside the sandbox lands in `~/.claude/projects/<hash>/`.
- Verify Anthropic OAuth works (the bind-mounted `~/.claude/.credentials.json` authenticates correctly).
- Compare the bwrap implementation cost / surface area against the unshare-based design previously sketched.
- Decide: adopt bwrap as Phase 1 primitive (and which other Phase 1 children are obviated), or fall back to unshare with policy-flipped defaults.

## Affected Phase 1 children (must wait on this evaluation)

- ClaudeConfig-40s.1 (claude-sandbox wrapper script) — wrapper shape changes substantially
- ClaudeConfig-40s.2 (privileged setup + sudoers) — likely unnecessary under bwrap user namespaces
- ClaudeConfig-40s.4 (default profile + ACLs) — YAML structure changes; ACLs may be unnecessary
- ClaudeConfig-40s.5 (Makefile install target) — install map changes
- J121-ft3 (claude-session user provisioning) — likely unnecessary

## Decisions to record

- DEC-006: allow-list policy as the governing principle for sandbox visibility (regardless of the bwrap-vs-unshare outcome).
- DEC-007 (only if bwrap is adopted): bubblewrap as Phase 1 sandbox primitive.

## Non-goals

- Don't evaluate Docker/Podman/systemd-nspawn here. Containers add host-tooling integration cost and per-invocation startup latency that's poorly suited to wrapping every `claude` invocation. Tradeoffs already explored 2026-05-05.
close_reason: Evaluation complete. bwrap validated against all four acceptance criteria (allow-list, OAuth, memory write-back, env hygiene). Decision recorded in DEC-007: adopt bwrap as Phase 1 sandbox primitive. Reference POC persisted at .tasks/40s.7-bwrap-eval/poc-wrapper.sh. Two open follow-ups (user-model decision, New Relic telemetry compliance) flagged in DEC-007 for separate evaluation.

title: Initial default profile YAML + ACLs on ~/.claude/projects/
description: Reference default sandbox profile (sandbox/profiles/default.yaml) — baseline visibility, no extra paths exposed. ACLs on ~/.claude/projects/<hash>/ granting claude-session write access (set up here, verified again in Phase 4).
notes:
Re-scoped 2026-05-05 per DEC-007. Original scope included ACLs for ~/.claude/projects/<hash>/ — under bwrap with same-UID, no ACLs are needed (sandbox runs as the user). If the user-model follow-up adopts a separate UID (path A), ACLs return to scope and J121-ft3 is reanimated. Until then, this bead delivers only the profile YAML (allow-list shape per DEC-006).
Re-expanded 2026-05-05 per DEC-007 follow-up #1 resolution (A2 adopted). ACL portion is back in scope; the profile YAML now references the bound paths under both hactar and claude-session homes. Implementation: (a) sandbox/profiles/default.yaml with read_only/read_write bind specs + UID/GID for the sandbox user, (b) sandbox/scripts/setup-claude-session-acls.sh providing setfacl-R + default-ACL idempotent setup, invoked by J121-ft3's provisioning script.
close_reason: Profile YAML + ACL setup script delivered. Profile structure anticipates Phase 2's formal schema (a92.1). ACL script is idempotent, has filesystem-support probe, fail-clean on missing user. End-to-end testing of the ACLs requires J121-ft3 (claude-session user); deferred. Validated via make install-test.

title: Makefile install target wired up to deploy Phase 1 components
description: Per DEC-004: marker-block managed for ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, three-way prompt for ~/.claude/settings.json, direct install for /usr/local/{bin,sbin}/, /etc/sudoers.d/, ~/.config/claude-sandbox/profiles/. Timestamped backup before any modification. Idempotent re-runs. make uninstall restores backups.
notes:
Install map narrowed 2026-05-05 per DEC-007. No /etc/sudoers.d/ entry, no /usr/local/sbin/ privileged helper. Just /usr/local/bin/claude-sandbox, ~/.config/claude-sandbox/profiles/*.yaml, and the marker-block update to ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md. If the user-model follow-up reintroduces a separate user, useradd + ACL setup re-enter the install steps.
Install map re-expanded 2026-05-05 per A2 user-model. Steps: (a) /usr/local/bin/claude-sandbox wrapper (direct), (b) ~/.config/claude-sandbox/profiles/*.yaml (direct), (c) ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md marker block (managed), (d) one-time provisioning hook calling J121-ft3's script (useradd + subuid + ACLs). NO /etc/sudoers.d/ entry, NO /usr/local/sbin/ privileged helper.
close_reason: Makefile installer delivered. Deploys wrapper to /usr/local/bin/, profile to ~/.config/claude-sandbox/profiles/, ACL script to /usr/local/share/claude-sandbox/scripts/. install/install-test/uninstall/verify/help targets. PREFIX-overridable. End-to-end smoke test passed (install-test deployed, wrapper ran via override, uninstall cleaned up).

title: Privileged namespace setup script + sudoers rule
description: Root-only namespace setup at sandbox/sbin/claude-sandbox-priv with NOPASSWD sudoers rule at sandbox/etc/sudoers.d/claude-sandbox. Owns the unshare --mount invocation and shadow-tmpfs mounts of credential paths. Must live in /usr/local/sbin/ (root-owned, user-non-writable) per Common Pitfall #1.
notes: Deferred 2026-05-05 per DEC-007. bwrap is rootless; sudoers + privileged helper are not needed under same-UID. May re-emerge in modified form if user-model follow-up adopts a separate UID via real account. See DEC-007 open follow-up #1.

title: claude-sandbox wrapper script
description: User-invokable entry-point at sandbox/bin/claude-sandbox. Calls sudo /usr/local/sbin/claude-sandbox-priv for privileged namespace setup, then drops to claude-session and execs claude. Single-profile, single-worktree, single-namespace per invocation.
notes:
Implementation shape clarified 2026-05-05 per DEC-007. Wrapper is a thin shell over bwrap (~25 lines of bind specifications). Reference POC at .tasks/40s.7-bwrap-eval/poc-wrapper.sh. Production version reads the bound profile YAML (40s.4) instead of inlining the bind list, and conditionally drops UID if the user-model follow-up adopts a separate user.
Implementation shape narrowed 2026-05-05 per DEC-007 + A2 user-model. Wrapper invocation gains --uid <claude-session-uid> --gid <claude-session-gid> (UIDs read from a static install-time config or /etc/passwd lookup). Cross-user binds: --ro-bind hactar's ~/.claude/{skills,agents,commands,settings.json} → /home/claude-session/.claude/{...}; --bind hactar's ~/.claude/projects/ → /home/claude-session/.claude/projects/ (writable via ACL); --bind /home/claude-session/.claude/.credentials.json (claude-session's own OAuth).
close_reason: claude-sandbox wrapper delivered. Reads profile YAML, translates to bwrap flags, exec's. Same-UID degraded fallback when claude-session doesn't exist. Verified: --version, claude -p OAuth round-trip, allow-list (8 credential paths invisible), env hygiene (0 credentials), home view shows only bind-mounted paths. A2 user-mapped mode requires J121-ft3 to test.

title: Phase 1: claude-sandbox wrapper
description:
Single-namespace, single-profile, single-worktree sandbox wrapper. Universal sandboxing for serial use. See docs/ROADMAP.md#phase-1.

Acceptance:
- claude-sandbox --version confirms wrapper + namespace + claude
- claude-sandbox -p "echo hello" succeeds
- Inside session: credential paths are empty (shadow-tmpfs), env shows no SNOWFLAKE_PRIVATE_KEY/JIRA_API_TOKEN, ~/.claude/ is read-only bind mount, ~/Source/claude-config/ is writable worktree

title: Provision claude-session user + /etc/subuid + ACLs (A2 user-model)
description:
One-shot provisioning script that creates the claude-session user and sets ACLs on ~/.claude/projects/<hash>/ for write-back from sandboxed sessions.

Lives in this repo at sandbox/scripts/provision-claude-session.sh and is invoked by the Makefile install target (see DEC-004). The privileged-namespace setup script lives elsewhere (sandbox/sbin/claude-sandbox-priv); this bead covers user creation + ACLs only.

Acceptance:
- claude-session user exists with appropriate UID/GID
- ACLs grant claude-session write access to ~/.claude/projects/<hash>/
- Idempotent re-runs do not duplicate state
- Idempotent backout supported (make uninstall removes the user + ACLs cleanly)
notes:
Deferred 2026-05-05 per DEC-007. bwrap with same-UID inside the namespace makes a separate claude-session user account unnecessary as a structural requirement. May re-emerge in modified form if user-model follow-up adopts a separate UID via real account (path A). See DEC-007 open follow-up #1.
Revived 2026-05-05 per DEC-007 follow-up #1 + user direction (path A2: rootless bwrap + named claude-session UID via subuid). Implementation:
1. useradd -r -d /home/claude-session -s /usr/sbin/nologin claude-session (system account, no shell)
2. /etc/subuid line granting hactar the right to map to claude-session's UID range
3. /etc/subgid analog for GID
4. setfacl -R -m u:claude-session:rwx ~/.claude/projects/ + default ACL for new files
5. mkdir -p /home/claude-session/.claude/ owned by claude-session for OAuth + .claude.json
6. Documented first-run ceremony: claude-session does its own browser OAuth flow on first sandbox invocation
Idempotent. make uninstall reverses all of it.
close_reason: provision-claude-session.sh delivered on phase1/wrapper-profile-makefile (commit 1064378). Wired into Makefile as separate provision/unprovision targets (kept out of install since they mutate system state). All acceptance criteria verified end-to-end against the live host: (1) user created uid=999 gid=995 nologin /home/claude-session; (2) /etc/subuid + /etc/subgid mappings hactar:999:1 / hactar:995:1; (3) setfacl rwx + default ACL on ~/.claude/projects/ via delegated setup-claude-session-acls.sh; (4) idempotent re-run is no-op; (5) --uninstall cleanly removes user, home, subuid/subgid lines, and named ACL entries; (6) re-provision after uninstall lands in working state. System left provisioned.

title: Hardening Slice B: Coordinator-primed gpg-agent (gpg-preset-passphrase)
description:
Wire the broker so it can decrypt credentials when the GPG key has a passphrase, without exposing the passphrase to the broker or persisting it on disk.

Design (per the conversation that produced this epic):
1. /home/claude-egress/.gnupg/gpg-agent.conf gains:
     allow-preset-passphrase
     max-cache-ttl 31536000
     default-cache-ttl 31536000
2. Coordinator (operator-side tooling) does:
     KEYGRIP=$(sudo -u claude-egress -H gpg --homedir /home/claude-egress/.gnupg \
                 --list-keys --with-keygrip --with-colons | awk -F: '/^grp/{print $10; exit}')
     pass show claude-config/egress-broker-gpg-passphrase \
       | sudo -u claude-egress -H env GNUPGHOME=/home/claude-egress/.gnupg \
             /usr/lib/gnupg/gpg-preset-passphrase --preset "$KEYGRIP"
3. Broker's ExecStartPre runs a sentinel-decrypt health check; systemd refuses
   to start the service if gpg-agent's cache is cold.
4. 'systemctl restart claude-egress-broker' evaporates the agent cache;
   coordinator workflow is prime → start, never start without prime.

Trust model: at-rest compromise requires BOTH (a) filesystem snapshot of
/home/claude-egress AND (b) operator's personal GPG key.

UNBLOCKS the broker's operational use after the current key-generation step
(see bd memory: claude-egress-broker-passphrase-gap-the-claude-egress).

Parent: ClaudeConfig-zvw

title: Hardening Slice A: Yubikey GPG slot for claude-egress
description:
Move the claude-egress GPG key to a Yubikey slot using 'gpg --edit-key <keyid>' → 'keytocard'. Private key no longer sits on disk; broker's pass-per-request workflow unchanged.

Operator-side prerequisites on WSL2:
- usbipd-win installed on Windows; 'usbipd bind --busid <id> --force' (one-time)
- Task Scheduler entry at logon: 'usbipd attach --busid <id> --wsl'
- pcscd running in WSL2: 'sudo systemctl enable --now pcscd'
- 'pcsc_scan' is the diagnostic for token visibility from the WSL2 side

Acceptance: 'pass show claude-egress/anthropic/api-key' as claude-egress succeeds
when the token is inserted (with operator touch if touch-required policy is set),
fails cleanly with operator-visible error when token is absent.

Parent: ClaudeConfig-zvw

title: Egress broker hardening: Yubikey, agent-primed passphrase, TPM-sealed creds, threat model
description:
Multi-slice hardening of the egress broker's credential path. Recommended order is cost-ascending:

SLICE A — Yubikey GPG slot (P2, operational + docs):
- Move claude-egress GPG key to a Yubikey slot via gpg --edit-key keytocard
- Document WSL2 USB passthrough prerequisite (usbipd-win bind --force + Task
  Scheduler attach at logon; pcscd running in WSL2)
- Verify: pass-per-request works against Yubikey, optional touch-required policy

SLICE B — Coordinator-primed gpg-agent (P2, design + small impl):
- claude-egress gpg-agent.conf gains allow-preset-passphrase + max-cache-ttl
- Coordinator (operator's session-startup tooling) decrypts the passphrase from
  operator's personal pass store and pipes it to gpg-preset-passphrase
- ExecStartPre health check: throwaway gpg --decrypt against a sentinel file;
  systemd refuses to start if agent cache is cold
- Trust model: 2-factor compromise (filesystem snapshot AND operator's personal
  GPG key) required to recover claude-egress credentials at rest
- Unblocks the broker after the operator-provisioned-passphrase state set by
  the current key-generation step. See bd memory: claude-egress-broker-passphrase-gap-the-claude-egress

SLICE C — systemd-creds + TPM-sealed credential (P3, design):
- Coordinator pipes unlocked passphrase to systemd-creds encrypt -, writes
  TPM-or-host-sealed credential
- Service unit gains LoadCredentialEncrypted=passphrase:...
- ExecStartPre primes gpg-agent from $CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY/passphrase
- Adds TPM-binding (sealed credential useless after TPM reset or on another host)
- Alternative path to Slice B; mostly orthogonal — pick one or document both

SLICE D — Threat-model document (P3, docs):
- Matrix: at-rest disclosure / live-process compromise / operator-error /
  supply-chain attack × pass-per-request / in-memory cache / agent-cached /
  Yubikey
- Concrete per-profile recommendation
- Explicit non-goal: 'add an in-memory cache to the broker' rejected with reasoning
- Result lands in docs/architecture/ (new file: egress-broker-threat-model.md)

Discovered-from ClaudeConfig-1ic. Each slice gets its own child bead.

title: Egress broker: age backend alongside pass
description:
Add an age-based (filippo.io/age) credential backend implementing the existing credentials.Backend interface in sandbox/broker/internal/credentials/. The 'credential.backend: age' policy value becomes valid; operators choose between 'pass' (GPG + agent + hardware-token path) and 'age' (Go-native, no subprocess, no daemon).

Trade-offs to document in the policy README:
- age: simpler, faster (no fork+exec), no hardware-token support, broker holds the unlocking key in process memory
- pass: subprocess overhead, hardware-token compatible, separate-process credential cache (gpg-agent)

Tests should mirror the existing pass+stub coverage. No wire-protocol change.

Discovered-from ClaudeConfig-1ic (the Python-Go consistency constraint that forced pass is gone now that the Python ref is frozen).

title: ciw.2 slices 6-7: Go production broker + freeze Python reference
description:
Slices 6-7 of ClaudeConfig-ciw.2 carved out as a follow-up after slices 1-5 merged to main (commits 932d5a4..b77c8c5).

Slice 6 - Go production broker:
- Port the Python reference (src/claude_config/egress_broker/) to Go
- Install path per DEC-029: sandbox/bin/claude-egress-broker
- Same wire protocol, same policy.yaml semantics, same SO_PEERCRED gate
- Use Type=notify with sd_notify (Python could not; Go can via coreos/go-systemd or hand-rolled)
- Update systemd unit ExecStart to point at the Go binary; the Python ref stays available via a drop-in for parity testing
- Tests: Go stdlib testing + integration tests against the same fixtures the Python tests use

Slice 7 - Freeze Python reference:
- Move src/claude_config/egress_broker/ -> sandbox/reference/egress-broker-python/
- Add NOTICE.md noting frozen-at-commit status (record the commit sha at freeze time)
- Decide whether to keep the Python ref in mypy/ruff coverage or exclude (probably exclude post-freeze)
- Move tests accordingly; either freeze them too or keep them running as a parity oracle

Acceptance: Go broker passes wire-protocol parity tests against the Python ref's fixtures; systemd unit runs the Go binary end-to-end on this host.

title: Polyglot host-side tool versioning via mise; Go toolchain provisioning
description:
Introduce mise as the source of truth for host-side dev tool versions, including the first Go toolchain pin for the Phase 1.5 egress broker (ClaudeConfig-ciw.2). Scope is host-side tooling only; claude-session runtime provisioning (currently apt-based for Lua, heredoc-built for Node/srt) is out of scope for this bead — see follow-up.

Scope:
- mise.toml (committed) with precise patch-pinned versions for: python, go, lua (luajit), cue, shellcheck, shfmt, stylua, bats.
- mise.local.toml.example (committed); mise.local.toml gitignored.
- enter hook that runs mise install -q, uv sync --quiet, and seeds .luarocks/ with lyaml + lua-cjson + busted (via luarocks tool from the luajit install).
- Justfile recipes continue working unchanged — mise activation puts the tools on PATH. No just→mise task migration; tasks remain in just.
- Retire host-side tool installs from sandbox/scripts/install-dev-tools.sh where mise now covers them (stylua, shfmt, lua-language-server). The script stays for any uncovered items + for claude-session provisioning.
- Pre-commit hook + just check continue to pass with mise-provided tools.
- Document bootstrap in CLAUDE.md (mise install on first clone).

Acceptance:
- Fresh checkout: mise install → just check is green without any apt-installed host tooling beyond a C toolchain + libyaml-dev (for luarocks-built lyaml/lua-cjson).
- Go binaries build via the mise-installed go.
- All existing justfile recipes pass under mise-managed tools.

Refs: DEC-019 (Python via uv); DEC-022 (per-language quality gates).
Hard-blocks ClaudeConfig-ciw.2 (broker needs Go toolchain pinned).
notes: Implemented. mise.toml committed with precise patch pins for python, go, luajit, shellcheck, shfmt, stylua, lua-language-server, cue, bats. Project-local .luarocks/ tree seeded via [hooks.enter] (busted, lyaml, lua-cjson) using apt's luarocks pointed at mise's luajit. mise [env] exports LUA_PATH + LUA_CPATH + .luarocks/bin on PATH so just/pre-commit/busted resolve everything. just dev-tools recipe retired (mise install supersedes); install-dev-tools.sh retained for claude-session step 7b. mise.local.toml gitignored; mise.local.toml.example committed. CLAUDE.md updated with bootstrap step. All gates green (ruff+mypy+ruff-fmt+shellcheck+stylua+shfmt+lua-language-server+pytest+busted). Branch feat/mise-go-toolchain, commit c5ddd17. ciw.2 unblocked.
close_reason: Acceptance verified end-to-end. mise install resolves all 9 tools; just check + pre-commit gates green; go run works via mise exec; .luarocks/ seeding succeeds. Unblocks ClaudeConfig-ciw.2.

title: Sidecar git: schema + env-var injection + chain_project_hooks toggle
description:
Gated on DEC-023 sidecar bead family (ClaudeConfig-wy9.*). Extends the
sidecar schema with project-local git config + git-hooks-chain semantics
for claude-session.

Schema addition (in schemas/sidecar.cue when that lands):
  git:
    user_name: string | *""
    user_email: string | *""
    require_signoff: bool | *false
    signing_key: string | *""   # GPG key fingerprint
    chain_project_hooks: bool | *false

Wrapper integration:
- At session boot, read the sidecar's git: block.
- Inject GIT_AUTHOR_NAME / GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL / GIT_COMMITTER_NAME /
  GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL via profile.environment.set (or via the wrapper's
  SCRUB_ENV for the standalone path).
- If chain_project_hooks: true, set up a hook-chain shim: each
  claude-config hook delegates to its project counterpart (if exists)
  after its own logic completes. Implementation TBD when bi0.2's decide()
  is extracted.

Acceptance:
- Sandboxed git commit picks up the sidecar's user_email value.
- With chain_project_hooks: true, a project's pre-commit fires AFTER
  claude-config's pre-commit (sequenced).

title: scdoc-based manpage scaffold: per-domain man/ + build-man + install-man recipes
description:
Foundation 4 of 5 — per ~/.claude/plans/quizzical-nibbling-crab.md.

Adopt scdoc for manpages, matching the pattern in
~/.local/src/wsl-vpn-namespace (separate .scd source files in man/
subdirs; Markdown architecture docs remain independent because their
content differs structurally from manpage reference content).

Deliverables:
- apt-install scdoc on host + claude-session (small C binary).
- Create sandbox/man/ with one worked example:
  sandbox/man/claude-sandbox.1.scd — reference for the wrapper. Cover
  SYNOPSIS, DESCRIPTION (terse), OPTIONS, ENVIRONMENT, EXIT STATUS,
  EXAMPLES, SEE ALSO. ~80-120 lines.
- Per-domain Justfile recipes:
  - build-man: scans the domain's man/ dir for .scd; runs `scdoc < x.N.scd
    > $build_dir/x.N` for each (using just's file-dep tracking if useful).
  - install-man: copies built manpages to
    ${PREFIX}/share/man/man<section>/; gzips them (optional; man(1)
    handles either).
- Root `just build-man` aggregates across domains.
- Root `just install-man` aggregates across domains.

Acceptance:
- `just build-man` produces sandbox/man/claude-sandbox.1 (groff source).
- `just install-man PREFIX=/tmp/test` lands /tmp/test/share/man/man1/
  claude-sandbox.1.
- `man -l /tmp/test/share/man/man1/claude-sandbox.1` renders sensibly.
- One scdoc worked example exists (above).

Subsequent F-doc2 migrates the existing architecture .md docs into
domain dirs + adds install-docs recipes for the Markdown side (separate
from manpages).
notes: Closed 2026-06-01: sandbox/man/claude-sandbox.1.scd worked example + sandbox/justfile build-man-sandbox/install-man-sandbox/uninstall-man-sandbox recipes + root just build-man/install-man/uninstall-man aggregators + .gitignore for built artifacts. man -l renders cleanly. install-man with PREFIX override lands at PREFIX/share/man/man1/. scdoc already apt-installed (sibling tool from wsl-vpn-namespace). Pattern ready for F-doc2 to add more domain manpages.

title: Full LuaCATS annotations on every hook + lib; strict-mode enforced via pre-commit
description:
Per the approved plan, after F-fmt1 + bi0.2 land the final API surface,
retrofit comprehensive LuaCATS annotations onto every existing hook +
shared lib. NOT the user's personal-config light-touch baseline — this is
production-quality code and warrants strict typing.

Scope:
- claude/scripts/hooks/_lib.lua: @class for any record shapes; @param /
  @return on every public M.* function (now_iso, normalize_path,
  match_paths, parse_kv_args, journal, emit_hook_output, append_jsonl,
  is_main). @alias for "warning"|"info"|"err"-style enum unions.
- claude/scripts/hooks/_git.lua: same coverage on current_branch,
  origin_head_branch, run.
- claude/scripts/hooks/config-guard.lua: WRITE_TOOLS as @class field +
  fully-typed decide(), is_protected(), normalize().
- claude/scripts/hooks/audit-event.lua: build_record(), journal_line(),
  cache_root(); @class for the record shape + parsed-input shape.
- claude/scripts/hooks/git-guard.lua: SAFE_VERBS / SAFE_SUBFORMS tables,
  STATE_CHANGING_VERBS, plus full coverage on decide / classify_verb /
  find_git_verb_in_command / tokenize / split_commands / has_git /
  resolve_protected_branches / match_pattern / glob_to_lua_pattern.

Wire `lua-language-server --check` strict mode into the pre-commit gate
(via F-fmt1's hook + .luarc.json). Un-annotated public APIs fail the
check.

Acceptance:
- `just lua-check` clean across all hook + lib files in strict mode.
- pre-commit blocks an attempted commit that introduces an un-annotated
  M.* public function.

title: Split SANDBOX_GUIDE.md across docs/architecture/ + docs/guides/
description:
Per the 2026-06-01 docs-scope discussion: SANDBOX_GUIDE.md currently
mixes three audiences (architecture, operator, dev). Split into the
structure established by Bead A.

Section migration:
  Mental model                       → architecture/sandbox-model.md
  Permission prompts vs OS-level     → architecture/sandbox-model.md
  Permission mode policy             → architecture/sandbox-model.md
  OAuth bootstrap and cwd safety     → guides/oauth-bootstrap.md
  Sandbox awareness for sessions     → guides/oauth-bootstrap.md
                                       (or new guides/sandbox-awareness.md
                                       if it grows)
  Hooks + supported-interpreter set  → architecture/hooks.md (Bead A;
                                       remove from SANDBOX_GUIDE)
  Recommend-and-execute pattern      → guides/agent-conventions.md
  Profile authoring                  → guides/profile-authoring.md
  Self-modification workflow         → guides/self-modification.md

Then delete SANDBOX_GUIDE.md; replace inbound links from CLAUDE.md /

files; each split target has the migrated content + an intro line

Depends on Bead A. Blocks bi0.1.
notes: Closed 2026-06-01: SANDBOX_GUIDE.md split into architecture/sandbox-model.md (3 substantive sections) + 5 guides/* files (oauth-bootstrap with full content; agent-conventions / profile-authoring / self-modification as stubs; troubleshooting with current Phase-1 entries + Phase-specific notes sub-section). Source deleted. Inbound links updated in README + CLAUDE + DECISION_LOG (DEC-004 + DEC-012 inline rename-notes) + architecture/README.

title: Doc reorg: docs/architecture/ + docs/usage/ + docs/specs/; document 40s.18/40s.19
description:
Per the 2026-06-01 docs-scope discussion: reorganize docs/ into

Reorg moves:
- Create docs/architecture/ — "how the system is designed, now."

  (scope is upstream srt + /sandbox, NOT the augmentation wrapper).

New content (extracted from session diagnostics + memories):

DEC-015 amended with rename note. DEC-025 + survey doc inbound links updated. hooks.md captures all 4 deployment subtleties from 40s.18 + 40s.19 diagnostics. SANDBOX_GUIDE split deferred to follow-up bead 8so.

title: Sidecar declarative-mode implementation (Python deps + system tools)
description:
Per DEC-023: handle setup.mode = "declarative":

- declarative.python.source = "pyproject.toml" → run `uv sync` (or
  equivalent) to populate the per-project venv from the project's
  pyproject.
- declarative.python.source = "requirements.txt" → `pip install -r`.
- declarative.system_tools: check availability; warn if missing.
- declarative.env: exported into the sandboxed session's environment.

Idempotent: re-running on an existing venv updates only what changed.

Depends on coordinator integration (3.3).

title: Sidecar script-mode implementation (imperative setup escape-hatch)
description:
Per DEC-023: handle setup.mode = "script":

- script.exec: relative path to an executable script in the project's
  .claude-session/ directory.
- The script runs as claude-session, with PWD = the project worktree,
  HOME = claude-session's home, the per-project venv path as
  $CLAUDE_SESSION_PROJECT_VENV, and the project's expected toolchain
  on PATH.
- Exit code 0 = success; non-zero = abort session boot with diagnostic.

Depends on coordinator integration (3.3).

title: Design sidecar Cue schema (declarative + script + Nix modes)
description:
Per DEC-023 + DEC-024: design schemas/sidecar.cue covering the three
setup modes.

Deliverables:
- schemas/sidecar.cue with #Sidecar definition:
  - setup.mode: "declarative" | "script" | "nix"
  - declarative.python: { source, extras }
  - declarative.system_tools: [...string]
  - declarative.env: { [string]: string }
  - script.exec: relative path to executable
  - nix.flake: relative path; nix.attr: string
  - egress_allowlist_additions: [...string] (feeds DEC-013)
  - Constraints: egress additions must be subset of system allowlist;
    exactly-one mode populated.
- A test fixtures directory with at least one valid example per mode
  and a few invalid examples (for `cue vet` smoke).
- docs/design/sidecar-schema.md describing the shape + rationale.

Refs: DEC-023, DEC-024.
notes: Schema design picks up the synthesis from 2026-06-02 design discussion. Refer to DEC-027 (config location, shape, step execution model), DEC-028 (shellHooks via mise), and DEC-026 (four-category task-runner taxonomy). bd memories: sidecar-config-location-and-shape, sidecar-step-execution-model, sidecar-shell-hooks-via-mise, sidecar-category-c-exposure-allowlist, sidecar-just-vs-mise-roles, sidecar-four-category-taxonomy. Acceptance criterion: schema in schemas/sidecar.cue validates the worked example YAML from DEC-027 cleanly, and the migration of bi0.6's project-local git-guard config to hooks.gitGuard.* under the new location is part of this work.

title: Sidecar coordinator integration (read + per-project venv + bind into sandbox)
description:
Per DEC-023: at session boot, the wrapper reads the active project's
`.claude-session/sidecar.yaml`, validates it against schemas/
sidecar.cue, and sets up the claude-session-owned per-project venv at
`~/.cache/claude-session/projects/<project-hash>/venv/`. The venv (or
its equivalent for non-Python projects) is bind-mounted into the
sandbox view.

Deliverables:
- Wrapper hook (in sandbox/bin/claude-sandbox) that:
  1. Locates `<project>/.claude-session/sidecar.yaml`.
  2. Runs `cue vet` against schemas/sidecar.cue (fail-loud on schema
     violations).
  3. Dispatches to the appropriate mode handler.
- The per-project venv path is hashed deterministically (so the same
  project re-uses its venv across sessions).
- Profile YAML gains a bind for the per-project venv into the sandbox
  view.
- Egress allowlist additions are merged into the active profile's
  allowlist for the session.

Depends on the schema (3.2). Per-mode implementations are separate
children (3.4, 3.5, 3.6) so this bead lands the wiring without
implementing all three modes at once.

Refs: DEC-023.

title: Cue infrastructure: install Cue host + claude-session; integrate cue vet

title: Per-project Claude-session sidecar (declarative + script + Nix modes)
description:
Per DEC-023: per-project Claude-session sidecar config at
`<project>/.claude-session/sidecar.yaml` declares the tooling a
sandboxed session needs to do real project work, with three supported
setup modes (declarative, script, Nix). Cue is the schema/validation
layer (DEC-024).

Priority P2 (not deferred): design now, implementation when the
immediate Phase 1 chain settles. No release pressure; conceptual
correctness is the ordering criterion.

Children:
- Cue infrastructure (install + integration)
- Sidecar Cue schema design
- Coordinator integration (sidecar read + per-project venv + bind)
- Declarative-mode implementation

Refs: DEC-023, DEC-024.

title: Provision: pipx/uv-tool-install the packaged Python tools for claude-session

Per DEC-019: add a step to provision-claude-session.sh that installs

Pattern follows the Node/srt provisioning (40s.15.11): heredoc-piped
sudo -u claude-session bash -s --; idempotent on version match;

Acceptance:

Depends on the uv scaffold (2.1) so there's a package to install.

title: Add bats; persist tests; wire per-language test layout

Per DEC-022: per-language native test frameworks, orchestrated by Just.

Deliverables:

Full just check gate clean.

title: Extend smoke-test.sh with multi-interpreter availability check
description:
Per DEC-020, add a section to sandbox/scripts/smoke-test.sh that
verifies each supported interpreter is callable as claude-session in

check is meaningful).
