Single sudo block covers both composed + standalone modes (binary resolution is identity-determined, not mode-determined). Full smoke: 10 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped.

title: Provision srt (+ node runtime) for claude-session — composed-mode prerequisite
description:
Found via 40s.15.4 smoke testing: composed mode fails with 'srt: not found'. srt is installed only in hactar's ~/.npm-packages/bin (0700 home, unreachable by claude-session). Same class as DEC-016 (claude install): composed mode runs 'srt claude' AS claude-session against the real fs, so srt must be on claude-session's real PATH.

Scope:
1. Install @anthropic-ai/sandbox-runtime for claude-session. srt is a Node package, and claude-session has NO node (claude is a self-contained binary). So this needs either: (a) install a node runtime + npm install -g srt for claude-session (its own ~/.npm or ~/.local), or (b) a SYSTEM srt install (/usr/local) reachable+executable by both users. Decide (system install avoids per-user node duplication; per-user matches DEC-016's claude-session-owns-its-tools model). Pin srt version (currently 0.0.52; NOT GA — see DEC-011 note).
2. Wire into provision-claude-session.sh (idempotent), version-pinned. Set DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER-equivalent if srt has one.
3. Ensure the wrapper's scrub PATH (already includes claude-session's ~/.local/bin + ~/.bun/bin as of 40s.15.4 fix) finds srt; adjust if srt lands elsewhere.

BLOCKS the composed half of 40s.15.4. Standalone mode is unaffected (no srt) and is fully smoke-validated. Open decision for the user: per-user node+srt for claude-session vs system-wide srt.
notes:
DECISION 2026-05-27: Per-user Node + srt for claude-session (NOT system-wide) — mirrors DEC-016's claude-session-owns-its-install model; self-contained principal. Scope: install a Node runtime + 'npm install -g @anthropic-ai/sandbox-runtime' (pinned 0.0.52) into claude-session's own home (~/.local or ~/.npm-global on its PATH); wire into provision-claude-session.sh (idempotent, version-pinned); the wrapper scrub PATH already includes claude-session's ~/.local/bin + ~/.bun/bin (40s.15.4 fix) — ensure srt lands on that PATH. Folds into the version-sync surface (40s.15.7) eventually. Unblocks composed mode + the composed half of 40s.15.4.
DONE 2026-05-27 (commit ea9e4be). Per-user Node (latest 22 LTS, v22.22.3, checksum-verified) + srt 0.0.52 installed into claude-session's ~/.local via provision step 6 (idempotent on srt pkg version; NODE_VERSION/SRT_VERSION pins; node+npm kept). Written-then-run: ran provision (installed), re-ran (skipped — idempotent). COMPOSED MODE NOW WORKS: smoke-test.sh 5/0/0, composed claude -p -> COMPOSED_OK. Decision: per-user (DEC-016 model), not system-wide.

title: git-guard C8: wire global PreToolUse hook into chezmoi modify_settings.json; chezmoi-manage script + config
description:
Phase 1 / cross-cutting. Depends on C1 (needs the script to point at). Without this the global hook is NEVER invoked. Source: ~/Source/claude-config-handoff.md §6; epic ClaudeConfig-bi0.

Add a PreToolUse entry with matcher:"Bash" pointing at ~/.claude/scripts/hooks/git-guard.py to chezmoi home/dot_claude/modify_settings.json (mirrors the existing Notification/SessionEnd wiring — file confirmed present). Ensure the hook script (home/dot_claude/scripts/hooks/executable_git-guard.py) + global config (home/dot_claude/git-guard.yaml) are chezmoi-managed and deploy cleanly via chezmoi apply (decision 3).

CURATION/OVERLAP (per epic): the wiring MUST be ADDITIVE. ClaudeConfig-40s.19 (audit hook suite, PreToolUse/PostToolUse) and 40s.14 (PreToolUse for spawn-agents) also register PreToolUse hooks in the same settings surface; Claude Code runs all of them (most-restrictive-wins). Do not clobber existing entries; coordinate the shared modify_settings.json PreToolUse list.
notes:
DEC-017 (2026-05-27): implementation language switched from Python to Lua. See ClaudeConfig-bi0 epic note. Blocked on ClaudeConfig-58n.1 (LuaJIT + lyaml + lua-cjson provisioning).
Closed 2026-06-01 as obsolete-by-foundations. Original scope: chezmoi-managed wiring of the global git-guard PreToolUse hook into Claude Code's settings.json. Post-foundation architecture has REPLACED this for sandboxed sessions: the wrapper's assemble_claude_dir consumes sandbox/scripts/_hooks-manifest.sh and writes the PreToolUse hook entries (including git-guard) into claude-session's ~/.claude/settings.json at every session boot. Adding a hook is a manifest edit (F-just1's pattern), not a chezmoi-modify_settings.json edit.

For the INTERACTIVE (non-sandboxed) Claude Code that hactar runs as their own user: that's a separate concern — chezmoi managing hactar's ~/.claude/settings.json. If desired, file a fresh follow-up bead scoped to 'extend chezmoi to insert the same hook entries hactar-side.' That's a chezmoi-repo concern, not claude-config's.

For sandboxed sessions (the primary git-guard target): wiring is done by F-just1 + bi0.2 + bi0.3 (config-guard, audit-event, git-guard all fire via _hooks-manifest.sh; live-verified in the bi0.2/bi0.3 closeout smokes).

title: git-guard C3: ref-rewriting always-ask denylist + push refspec target parsing (Subtlety 1)
description:
Phase 1. Depends on C1 (pairs with C2). Source: ~/Source/claude-config-handoff.md §5.2(2); epic ClaudeConfig-bi0.

Catch ref rewrites that bypass the current-branch check:
- Parse git push refspecs: <src>:<dst> or push to a `.` remote names an explicit destination branch → check THAT against the protected set; ask if protected.
- Always-ask denylist for HEAD-bypassing ref rewrites (ask regardless of current branch): branch -f; branch -D/-d of a protected branch; update-ref; push with explicit :dst refspec; push to `.`; fetch . <src>:<dst>.
- Do NOT attempt exhaustive refspec coverage — the always-ask denylist catches the dangerous tail with bounded effort. Never silently allow a risky verb that failed to parse (→ ask, handoff §6/§8).

Extends git-guard.py (C1). Default: ask (handoff §5.4).
notes:
DEC-017 (2026-05-27): implementation language switched from Python to Lua. See ClaudeConfig-bi0 epic note. Blocked on ClaudeConfig-58n.1 (LuaJIT + lyaml + lua-cjson provisioning).
Closed 2026-06-01: parse_push_refspec + analyze_push (3-valued: ask/allow/defer) + analyze_branch + ALWAYS_ASK_REF_REWRITE table + fetch-to-loopback detection + decide() restructure (ALWAYS_ASK > analyzer > STATE_CHANGING C2 > generic). 32 new busted cases (126 total). Live smoke confirms all the dangerous push/branch/update-ref/fetch-loopback variants ask; explicit-refspec push of feature ref from a protected current branch now correctly ALLOWS (C3 refspec override of C2 default).

title: git-guard C1: global hook scaffold + git-guard.yaml schema + safe-verb fast-allow
description:
Phase 1, FOUNDATIONAL — blocks all other git-guard children. Source: ~/Source/claude-config-handoff.md §3/§4/§5.1/§6; shared context in epic ClaudeConfig-bi0.

Stand up git-guard.py as a PreToolUse(matcher:Bash) hook:
- Parse stdin contract {cwd, tool_name, tool_input.command}. First action: cheap 'does the command contain a git invocation at all?' → if not, emit allow and exit immediately (runs on EVERY Bash call; must bail in ms).
- Classify safe verbs → fast-allow (log diff show status ls-files ls-tree rev-parse rev-list for-each-ref cat-file blame describe 'branch --list/--show-current' shortlog reflog 'config --get/--list' 'remote -v/show' symbolic-ref 'tag --list' grep).
- Gated verbs → emit allow/ask/deny JSON (hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision + human reason; exit 2 also blocks; exit 0 + no JSON = allow). Confirm exact schema vs current Claude Code hooks docs at implementation.
- Define + DOCUMENT the GLOBAL git-guard.yaml schema (handoff §4): protected_branches, per_repo (abs-path match, first-match-wins — decision 2), ask_branch_patterns, branch_naming{patterns,on_violation}, options{git_dash_c_policy, fallback_on_unparseable}. No protected-branch decision logic yet beyond plumbing (that's C2/C3).

Resolved decisions baked in: ONE SCRIPT/TWO MODES (5) — this same script serves project mode (C6); patterns = globs with optional `re:` prefix (1); pyyaml safe_load, on ImportError fail safe→ask with clear stderr (6). Python per the python-scripting skill (stdlib logging→stderr, argparse only if flags grow).

Deliverables (chezmoi-managed, decision 3):
- home/dot_claude/scripts/hooks/executable_git-guard.py → ~/.claude/scripts/hooks/git-guard.py
- home/dot_claude/git-guard.yaml → ~/.claude/git-guard.yaml
notes:
DEC-017 (2026-05-27): implementation language switched from Python to Lua. See ClaudeConfig-bi0 epic note. Blocked on ClaudeConfig-58n.1 (LuaJIT + lyaml + lua-cjson provisioning).
Closed 2026-06-01: git-guard.lua (foundation — has_git fast-bail, quote-aware tokenizer, compound-command splitting, env+flag-aware verb identification, SAFE_VERBS + SAFE_SUBFORMS classification matrix) + git-guard.yaml (full handoff §4 schema documented; C1 wires options only) + 26 busted cases + manifest + hooks-manifest wiring + docs/architecture/hooks.md table update. End-to-end smoke confirms allow on `git --version` inside standalone bwrap. Deployment-path deviation: config lives at ~/.claude/hooks/git-guard.yaml (co-located with hook + single mirror destination) rather than the bead's literal ~/.claude/git-guard.yaml — documented in the YAML header.

title: git-guard C2: config-driven protected-branch resolution + ask-on-protected
description:
Phase 1. Depends on C1. Source: ~/Source/claude-config-handoff.md §2/§5.2; epic ClaudeConfig-bi0.

Resolve a repo's protected branches in order: per_repo override (abs-path match) → global protected_branches → origin/HEAD (LAST-RESORT fallback only). Get current branch via git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD. On a state-changing write while current branch matches a protected pattern → ASK. Also ASK if current branch matches ask_branch_patterns (e.g. release/*).

BAKE IN the §2 finding: do NOT trust default-branch detection. The J121 repos prove it wrong (SmartStore_Base canonical=DEV though origin/HEAD=master & init.defaultBranch=main; SmartStore_PlugIns canonical=Prototype). Config is authoritative.

Patterns = globs with optional `re:` prefix (decision 1). Decision default: ask on violation (handoff §5.4). Extends git-guard.py (deliverable from C1).
notes:
DEC-017 (2026-05-27): implementation language switched from Python to Lua. See ClaudeConfig-bi0 epic note. Blocked on ClaudeConfig-58n.1 (LuaJIT + lyaml + lua-cjson provisioning).
Paused 2026-06-01: per the approved plan (~/.claude/plans/quizzical-nibbling-crab.md), bi0.2 waits behind 5 foundation beads (F-fmt1, F-just1, F-test1, F-git1, F-doc1) that restructure the Justfile, colocate tests, provision claude-session git identity + core.hooksPath dir, scaffold scdoc manpages, and add strict Lua type-checking. bi0.2 resumes after foundations land.
Closed 2026-06-01: _git.lua shared module + git-guard.lua C2 extension (STATE_CHANGING_VERBS classification + glob/regex pattern matcher + resolve_protected_branches chain per_repo→global→origin/HEAD + decide() integration). 5 new _git_spec cases + ~25 new git-guard C2 spec cases (busted 94 total, was 67). Live smoke confirms feature-branch commit allows + protected-branch / fail-safe paths ask. Foundation stands ready for bi0.3 (ref-rewriting denylist + refspec parsing).

title: git-guard PreToolUse hooks: balance git-permission convenience with safety
description:
Source spec: ~/Source/claude-config-handoff.md (read end-to-end; self-contained). Translate-only pass produced these beads; implementation is NOT started.

GUIDING PRINCIPLE (handoff §1): replace coarse git allow-rules (Bash(git:*)) with PreToolUse hooks that make a per-command allow/ask/deny decision balancing:
- Convenience: minimize prompts for safe git ops (reads; writes on a feature branch in a trusted dir) — normal feature work almost never interrupted.
- Safety: require permission (or deny) for dangerous/anomalous ops — writing a protected/canonical branch, rewriting refs out from under HEAD, git in an untrusted dir, git -C triggering repo-local hooks.
Permission RULES do pure textual glob-matching and can't see runtime state (branch, cwd); a PreToolUse hook can run git to inspect state and decide. Guardrail, not a sandbox (handoff §8): stops accidental/anomalous ops, not a determined adversary; prefer ask over silent-allow on uncertainty.

CRITICAL FINDING (handoff §2): protected branch is CONFIG-DRIVEN, not the 'default' branch. Live evidence: SmartStore_Base canonical=DEV (origin/HEAD=master, init.defaultBranch=main — both wrong); SmartStore_PlugIns canonical=Prototype. Auto-detecting a default branch would guard the wrong branch. origin/HEAD is last-resort fallback only.

ARTIFACTS chezmoi-managed; TRACKING here (handoff §6 split, same as poll_until.py / on-notification.sh). Each child records its chezmoi deliverable path.

RESOLVED Section 10 decisions (2026-05-27 ruling):
1. Pattern syntax = simple globs with optional `re:` prefix for regex.
2. per_repo.match / allowed_git_dirs key = ABSOLUTE PATH, first-match-wins; PLUS a git worktree whose main repo is an allowlisted path is ALSO allowed (project hook resolves via git rev-parse --git-common-dir/--show-toplevel) so agent-created worktrees (e.g. .worktrees/<bead> from /work-queue) need not be listed individually. Document the clone-elsewhere caveat.
3. Global git-guard.yaml = chezmoi-managed static file, edited in chezmoi source.
4. Project hook registration = LOCAL .claude/settings.local.json (NOT committed) — the hook references a user-specific path; committing would break Claude Code for collaborators; claude-init re-scaffolds per machine.
5. One script, two modes (global vs project by which config it finds / a flag).
6. pyyaml assumed present (6.0.2 verified in runtime); hook handles ImportError by failing safe (ask) with a clear stderr msg; verify availability in the claude-session sandbox runtime at implementation.

OVERLAP / coordination (curation flags):
- ClaudeConfig-40s.19 (audit hook suite, PreToolUse/PostToolUse) and 40s.14 (PreToolUse for spawn-agents) also register PreToolUse hooks. Claude Code runs ALL of them; most-restrictive-wins. Child 8's modify_settings.json wiring must be ADDITIVE (not clobber the existing Notification/SessionEnd or any other PreToolUse entries). Coordinate the shared hook-registration surface.
- Child 9 (retire redundant git allow-rules) overlaps the existing Bash(git:*) allow-rule and the broader permissions theme.

Labels: area:git-guard is the feature cluster (filter all with --label-pattern area:git-guard); area:claude + scope:security the spine; area:chezmoi on chezmoi-touching children; area:tooling on the claude-init child.
notes: DEC-017 (2026-05-27): the git-guard fast-path hook is language-switched from Python to Lua. Hot path is git-guard.lua under LuaJIT, with lyaml + lua-cjson for config + I/O. The handoff doc's Python-specific guidance (helper functions, refspec parser layout) carries over conceptually; the implementation is Lua. All implementation children (bi0.1-bi0.9) gain a blocking dependency on ClaudeConfig-58n.1 (Lua provisioning).

title: Harden claude-session launch cwd (inherited-cwd leak); controlled-cwd OAuth bootstrap
description:
SECURITY PITFALL found 2026-05-27: a 0700 home blocks PATH traversal but NOT an inherited cwd fd. sudo preserves cwd, so 'sudo -u claude-session ... claude' launched from inside hactar's home gave claude-session a cwd fd inside it; hactar's ~/.local, ~/.local/share, ~/.local/share/chezmoi are 0755, so claude-session could list+read them (verified: read AGENTS.md) despite the 0700 gate, because relative access from an inherited cwd never re-checks the parent chain. The real sandbox (claude-sandbox via bwrap/srt) is UNAFFECTED (namespace excludes hactar's home + --chdir to the bound project); this only bites bare/unsandboxed claude-session invocations.

EMPHASIS — installer & bootstrap must not fall into this trap:
1. PROVISIONING PATH (primary): the bootstrap/OAuth flow must NEVER launch claude-session's claude from an arbitrary (caller-inherited) cwd. Provide a dedicated controlled-cwd entry — e.g. 'claude-sandbox --oauth' (or a provision-script step) that runs claude-session's claude with cwd FORCED to claude-session's own home (or a neutral dir) AND the network scope needed for the login/OAuth domains (which the composed srt allowlist does not include). Replace the provision script's current 'Next: invoke claude-sandbox interactively...' guidance with this hardened path so no human is ever told to run bare claude from a random dir.
2. WRAPPER: confirm claude-sandbox always sets a safe cwd in BOTH modes (composed --chdir/standalone --chdir already do; add a guard/assert).
3. DOCS: add a Common Pitfall to CLAUDE.md + a SANDBOX_GUIDE note: '0700 home != protection for 0755 subdirs under inherited cwd; every claude-session invocation goes through the sandbox or a controlled cwd.'
4. OPTIONAL DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH: tighten perms on claude-session-relevant 0755 subdirs, or document that hardening hactar's ~/.local* perms is the user's call.

No data was leaked in the 2026-05-27 incident (operator paused at the trust prompt; nothing persisted). This bead prevents recurrence via tooling.
notes: DONE 2026-05-27 (this commit). (1) claude-sandbox --oauth: bare cwd-forced (SANDBOX_HOME) claude, no srt/bwrap (login domains off-allowlist), requires claude-session. (2) assert_safe_cwd guard: composed/standalone refuse home root + ~/.dotfile dirs (case $USER_HOME/.* ; project-relative .worktrees unaffected). (3) composed mode cd's to project deterministically. (4) provision script + Makefile guidance → claude-sandbox --oauth; CLAUDE.md pitfall #4 + SANDBOX_GUIDE 'OAuth bootstrap and cwd safety' + optional perms note. Validated: shellcheck clean; 3 modes dry-run; guard refuses ~/.config, skipped for oauth. The bash -c cwd pattern uses intentional single-quotes (SC2016 disabled).

title: Per-project ACL grant for active worktree; extend setup-claude-session-acls.sh + wire into launcher
description:
Gap found via the worktree-binding analysis (same 0700 root cause as DEC-016). The wrapper binds ${ACTIVE_PROJECT} but nothing ACL-grants it — setup-claude-session-acls.sh today only handles ~/.claude/projects, so a real project bind/access fails (claude-session can't read hactar's 0700 home).

Scope:
1. Extend setup-claude-session-acls.sh (or a launcher step) to, for the active project: setfacl -m u:claude-session:--x on the home + intermediate dirs (traversal-only, no read/list of the rest of the 0700 home), and setfacl -R -m u:claude-session:rwX + default ACL on the worktree itself.
2. Wire into the launcher host-side prep so the ACL is granted before the bind (standalone) / before exec (composed). Idempotent.
3. Both modes: composed relies on the kernel ACL (srt allowWrite already includes ${ACTIVE_PROJECT}); standalone --bind ${ACTIVE_PROJECT} resolves once readable.
4. Security: claude-session sees ONLY the granted project + traversal path, never the rest of the home (0700 default-deny preserved).

Phase 3/4 note: per-session worktrees may live outside hactar's home to shrink ACL surface (main repo .git still needs an ACL for git ops); Phase 4 path-aliasing mounts at the canonical path for memory continuity. This bead = the Phase 1 active-project ACL.
notes: DONE 2026-05-27 (this commit). setup-claude-session-acls.sh --project: traversal-only --x ACL through non-world-traversable ancestors (handles sticky 't' dirs; tolerant of un-ownable ancestors) + idempotent-fast recursive rwX + default on the worktree; projects_dir pass also made idempotent-fast. Launcher resolve_acl_script() + host-side-prep grant (real runs only, separate-user only, dry-run-guarded, non-fatal). Verified access matrix on a test tree: claude-session reads/writes project incl. nested, CANNOT read sibling secret, CANNOT list 0700 home. shellcheck clean. UNBLOCKS the project-access half of 40s.15.4.

title: Skill/agent/command sharing via launch-time assembly (baseline mirror)
description: Per DEC-016 (same 0700 root cause as the binary). claude-session can't read hactar's ~/.claude/skills, so the current profile binds of hactar's ~/.claude/{skills,agents,commands,scripts} are BROKEN under sudo -u. Replace with LAUNCH-TIME ASSEMBLY: the launcher (host-side, runs as hactar -> can read hactar's ~/.claude) copies hactar's ~/.claude/{skills,agents,commands,scripts} into a claude-session-readable staging dir (/tmp/claude-sandbox-claude.<uid>/..., world-readable; not secret), then binds the staging dir into the sandbox. No ACL holes in hactar's home (launcher does the reading). PHASE 1 SCOPE = baseline full mirror (fixes the broken binds; every session sees hactar's skills/agents/commands/scripts). Overlay/omit refinement is the Phase 2 child. Depends on 40s.15.6 (profile-binds rework).
notes:
SCOPE ADD (2026-05-27, from 40s.16): also deliver claude-session-scoped Claude Code permission deny rules — Read(**/.env), **/.env.*, **/*.pem, **/*.key, **/id_rsa*, **/credentials.json etc. — in claude-session's own ~/.claude/settings.json (assembled here). These use Claude Code's app-layer permission matcher where **/.env DOES work (unlike srt's Linux filesystem denyRead, which is absolute-only — see 40s.15.2/DEC-013). Scoped to claude-session (not host-wide) so hactar's interactive sessions are unaffected. The disableAutoMode lockoff is separate + host-wide (40s.16).
DONE 2026-05-27 (this commit). DEVIATION from handoff stage+bind: composed mode (srt) doesn't remap, so MIRROR into claude-session's own ~/.claude instead (serves both modes: composed direct, standalone sandbox_home bind). assemble_claude_dir(): host stages 4 dirs → /tmp (a+rX); claude-session rsync --delete into ~/.claude. Full mirror (Phase 1; omit/override = 40s.15.8.1). claude-session-scoped Read(**/.env)-family deny rules merged into its settings.json (env preserved). Profile sandbox_home gains the 4 dirs (ro). Validated: shellcheck; real mirror (skills:10/agents:1/commands:31/scripts:9); settings merge (env + 10 deny); standalone dry-run binds all 4.

title: Install Claude Code + bun for claude-session; fix profile claude-binds
description:
Per DEC-016. claude-session gets its OWN Claude Code + bun install (its own home), because composed mode (srt, no remap) needs claude on claude-session's real PATH and DEC-012's 0700 hactar home makes sharing hactar's binary impossible in both modes.

Scope:
1. Provisioning: extend provision-claude-session.sh (or sibling) to install Claude Code + bun as claude-session via sudo -u claude-session + official installer, version-pinned (match hactar's current, e.g. 2.1.150). claude install <version> / bun install.
2. Profile-binds fix: standalone profile's claude binds currently reference hactar's ~/.local/{bin,share}/claude + ~/.bun — BROKEN under sudo -u (claude-session can't read hactar's 0700 home). Repoint to claude-session's OWN paths, bound back over the tmpfs home.
3. Verify: sudo -u claude-session claude --version works (claude on its real PATH); standalone bwrap binds resolve.

Blocks 40s.3 (OAuth needs claude present). Head of the smoke-test dependency chain (install -> 40s.3 OAuth -> 40s.15.4).
notes: DONE 2026-05-27 (commit c5d477b). claude-session has its own Claude Code 2.1.150 install (verified: sudo -u claude-session claude --version → 2.1.150). DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER=1 set in claude-session's ~/.claude/settings.json. provision-claude-session.sh step 5 wires the install (pinned to host's version, idempotent). Profile binds repointed to claude-session's own ~/.local paths (sandbox_home); ~/.bun bind dropped (self-contained binary); hactar ~/.claude skill binds commented out pending 40s.15.8 assembly. Wrapper bug fixed: sandbox_path_exists() checks as the sandbox user (the -e check ran as hactar and skipped claude-session's 0750-home binds). NOTE: claude-session at 2.1.150, hactar drifted to 2.1.152 (auto-update) — exact ongoing sync is 40s.15.7. UNBLOCKS 40s.3 (OAuth: claude-session now has claude to run the flow). Pre-existing SC2046 warnings (lines 104/109, re-exec logic) unchanged — candidate cleanup.

title: claude-egress-broker: Unix-socket credential broker
description: Per DEC-013. Host-side daemon (runs as claude-egress) holding credentials from /etc/claude-config/credentials/; binds a Unix socket into the sandbox mount namespace; attaches the right credential per destination policy and makes the real upstream HTTPS call. Sandbox never holds credentials or speaks TLS to the real destination. Attribution via SO_PEERCRED. Language: Python or Go (HTTP/JSON).
notes: All 7 slices landed. Slices 1-5 directly; slices 6-7 via ClaudeConfig-1ic (Go broker + Python freeze).

title: claude-egress-proxy: SNI-inspecting passthrough proxy (domain allowlist)
description: Per DEC-013. Host-side proxy (runs as claude-egress) for uncredentialed but allowlisted endpoints: reads SNI from the ClientHello (plaintext), checks against the allowlist, splices encrypted bytes through or drops. No TLS termination, no CA cert in the sandbox. Resolves SNI->IP itself to defeat SNI-lying. ~300 LOC Go (crypto/tls.ClientHelloInfo + io.Copy). DELIVERS DOMAIN-ALLOWLISTING FOR BOTH MODES: standalone (bwrap can't filter domains) and a deterministic layer beneath srt's own proxy for composed (Register-bypass backstop). ECH will eventually obsolete SNI inspection (see follow-up tracker).

title: Provision claude-egress UID + policy/credential dirs
description: Per DEC-013. Add a dedicated claude-egress user (separate top-level UID, NOT a subuid of claude-session — a compromised claude-session must not tamper with proxy policy). Extend provision-claude-session.sh (or sibling). Create /etc/claude-config/egress-policy/ (root-owned 0640, claude-egress-group-readable) and /etc/claude-config/credentials/ (root-owned 0640). No sudoers entry for claude-egress.
notes: Provisioning script implemented and verified end-to-end: claude-egress user (uid 995, distinct top-level UID), /etc/claude-config/{egress-policy,credentials}/ at 0750 root:claude-egress, no sudoers, no subuid. Idempotent on re-run; --uninstall preserves operator data, --purge removes dirs. Wired into sandbox/justfile (provision-egress + chained provision/unprovision recipes). All quality gates green. Commit 35c214b on feat/egress-mediation-phase1.5.
close_reason: Acceptance verified; blocks ciw.2 / ciw.3 / ciw.4 now released.

title: Phase 1.5: egress mediation (broker + SNI proxy)
description: Per DEC-013. Deterministic egress mediation under a dedicated claude-egress UID, host-side, composing below srt/sandbox. Two mechanisms (Unix-socket credential broker; SNI-inspecting passthrough proxy) + hardening follow-ups. Closes the bypass class from the 2026-05-20 Register disclosure by enforcing destination-by-credential and destination-by-SNI deterministically. The SNI proxy also provides domain-allowlisting for standalone mode (bwrap can't filter domains) and a deterministic layer beneath srt's own proxy for composed mode.
notes:
[2026-06-02 design discussion — security boundary clarification]

ciw.4 (nftables) is NOT optional. Without kernel-enforced egress restriction scoped to claude-session's UID, the broker and SNI proxy are convenience infrastructure, not security infrastructure — a malicious agent controlling claude-session can simply decline to use them and open direct sockets to whatever survives /sandbox's app-layer filter. ciw.4 (and/or ciw.5 net-namespace confinement) is what makes the mediation deterministic.

The full security claim of this epic is delivered only when ALL of {ciw.1 ✓, ciw.2 broker, ciw.3 SNI proxy, ciw.4 nftables} have landed. ciw.5 (netns) and ciw.6 (ECH tracking) are enhancement / future-proofing layers.

This epic does not close while ciw.4 is open — structural property of bead epics, but worth stating explicitly.

title: Two-mode smoke tests (composed + standalone)
description: Expand ClaudeConfig-40s.6 to cover both modes. Verify: claude --version and claude -p succeed in each mode; the 14 denyRead paths are blocked; off-allowlist egress is denied; on-allowlist egress works; env-scrub strips Anthropic creds; runs as claude-session UID. Depends on 40s.15.2 and 40s.15.3.
notes:
2026-05-26 (sandbox-wrapper queue): PARKED — operator-required. Full acceptance ('claude -p succeeds in each mode'; denyRead/env-scrub/uid checks inside a REAL sandboxed session) needs a working claude run, which needs claude-session's Anthropic OAuth token = 40s.3 (one-time interactive browser flow, operator action). Added dep on 40s.3. Pre-authoring the smoke-test script unrun would violate verification discipline (untested artifact), so deferred until OAuth lands — then author script + run all checks (composed + standalone) in one pass. Non-OAuth pre-checks already green from sibling work: --version (both modes), real bwrap → uid=999 (40s.15.3), emitter→srt-accepts (40s.15.2), dry-run exec lines (40s.15.1).
PARTIAL 2026-05-27. smoke-test.sh delivered (sandbox/scripts/, expands 40s.6). STANDALONE fully validated: claude -p -> STANDALONE_OK end-to-end; uid=999; hactar home invisible; env scrubbed (4 pass/0 fail). COMPOSED blocked: srt not reachable by claude-session (host npm-global is 0700) — depends on ClaudeConfig-40s.15.11 (provision srt for claude-session). Also fixed a scrub-PATH bug (claude-session bin dirs were missing → composed/oauth couldn't find claude). Re-run smoke-test.sh once ClaudeConfig-40s.15.11 lands to validate the composed half + close.
DONE 2026-05-27 (commit ea9e4be). BOTH modes validated via smoke-test.sh: 5 passed / 0 failed / 0 skipped. standalone + composed each: claude -p returns the sentinel end-to-end; uid 999; no other homes visible; env scrubbed. Composed unblocked by 40s.15.11 (Node+srt). Fixed two smoke-harness bugs found here (tail -1 vs trailing newline; env command-builtin pre-check).

title: Standalone bwrap-args builder (replicates srt fs/network policy)
description: For --standalone mode: translate profile YAML into bwrap arguments that replicate the filesystem allow-list (DEC-006) and network policy srt would otherwise enforce. Reuses the phase1-branch POC wrapper's bwrap arg construction as starting point; extends it to honor the same profile schema the settings emitter (40s.15.2) consumes, so both modes are driven by one profile. Includes WSL2 /mnt/wsl DNS bind (DEC-007 consequence). Depends on 40s.15.1.
notes: DONE 2026-05-26 (this commit). Builder validated: dry-run arg vector correct; real 'sudo -u claude-session bwrap ... -- id' → uid=999(claude-session) confirms rootless userns + binds. FS allow-list (DEC-006 via binds) + network on/off + WSL2 DNS done. Network domain-allowlisting is OUT of standalone-bwrap scope (no L7 in bwrap) — deferred to DEC-013 SNI proxy (ciw.3) via parity bead 40s.15.5 (related, non-blocking, so this bead closes in Phase 1). Profile = one file, two mode schemas (binds=standalone, composed:=srt).

title: Profile YAML to settings.json emitter (composed mode)
description: Emits the settings.json (and srt config) consumed by composed mode from the profile YAML. Must encode the invariants discovered in 40s.17/40s.20: baseline denyRead list (14 paths from quickstart), permissions.disableAutoMode:disable in managed settings, NEVER emit allowLocalBinding:true, NEVER emit a permissive permissionMode. The credential deny-list bead (40s.16) ships the baseline default.json this emitter merges/extends. Depends on 40s.15.1 (wrapper skeleton).
notes:
DONE 2026-05-26 on feat/40s.15-compose-upstream (commits 8aa3893 emitter+wiring, df4d392 docs). Emitter claude-sandbox-emit-srt-settings: profile composed: section → srt-settings.json with absolute narrow credential denyRead baseline + allowLocalBinding=false invariant; wired into wrapper composed path (generates to claude-session-readable /tmp). KEY FINDING: srt Linux denyRead is absolute-only, not glob — drove the absolute baseline and corrections to DEC-013 + quickstart + 40s.16. Validated: shellcheck, valid JSON, srt accepts (Zod), dry-run exec line correct. NOT validated: live composed claude run (needs 40s.3 OAuth + sudoers entry) — that's 40s.15.4.
GAP FIXED in 40s.16's commit: the emitter (claude-sandbox-emit-srt-settings) was not in the Makefile install map, so composed mode would break post-install (wrapper SELF_DIR couldn't find it). Added EMITTER_SRC/DST + install rule + INSTALLED_FILES entry; verified via make install-test (lands at BIN_DIR beside the wrapper).

title: Two-mode claude-sandbox wrapper skeleton (composed srt / --standalone bwrap)
description: Per DEC-011. The wrapper's outer shell: arg parsing (--standalone flag), host-side prep (identity switch via sudo -u claude-session, env-scrub), and exec dispatch to either 'srt claude' (composed, default) or 'bwrap <args> -- claude' (standalone). Consumes profile YAML for both paths but delegates the mode-specific config construction to 40s.15.2 (settings emitter) and 40s.15.3 (bwrap args builder). Reuses the phase1-branch POC wrapper as starting point. Foundational — blocks the hook beads (40s.18/19) and telemetry (40s.21) which need a running session.
notes:
DONE 2026-05-26 on branch feat/40s.15-compose-upstream (commit 08af8f3). Accumulator branched off main, phase1/wrapper-profile-makefile merged in (Makefile, POC wrapper, profiles, provision/ACL scripts).

Skeleton delivered: two-mode dispatch (composed srt default / --standalone bwrap), DEC-012 sudo -u claude-session identity switch (replaces POC --uid remap; degrades to same-UID + warning), env-scrub (env -i minimal allowlist + CLAUDE_CODE_SUBPROCESS_ENV_SCRUB for composed), --dry-run.

VALIDATED: shellcheck clean; --version (srt 1.0.0, claude 2.1.150); --dry-run both modes emits correct identity+exec lines. NOT validated (out of skeleton scope): end-to-end claude run — needs 40s.15.2 (srt config), 40s.3 (OAuth), and installed /etc/sudoers.d/claude-sandbox (currently absent on this box; general passwordless sudo masked it). That's the 40s.15.4 smoke test.

Bug found+fixed during validation: build_scrubbed_env's trailing `[cond] && action` made the function return nonzero in standalone mode, tripping set -e at the call site (the && exemption is lexical; the function's return propagates). Converted to if-form.

NOTE: srt reports 1.0.0, not the 0.0.52 beta the survey found — possible srt GA. Worth confirming for DEC-011's beta-stability assumption.
POST-CLOSE FIX (2026-05-26, commit 1c3d113 on feat/40s.15-compose-upstream): srt's config flag is -s/--settings (default ~/.srt-settings.json), NOT --config — the skeleton's composed invocation would have failed. Renamed CLAUDE_SANDBOX_SRT_CONFIG → CLAUDE_SANDBOX_SRT_SETTINGS, pass --settings explicitly. Also confirmed srt is NOT GA: package @anthropic-ai/sandbox-runtime is 0.0.52; CLI --version misreports 1.0.0 (hardcoded). DEC-011 beta stance documented + unchanged.

title: Document and verify /sandbox + srt quickstart guide (docs/guides/quickstart-sandbox.md)
description:
Quickstart guide drafted 2026-05-26 at docs/guides/quickstart-sandbox.md. Gets operators running Claude Code under /sandbox + srt today, before claude-config's augmentation layer (ClaudeConfig-40s.15) ships. Includes 14-path denyRead baseline, allowedDomains starter list, WSL2 DNS workaround, verification commands.

This bead: (a) end-to-end follow the guide from a fresh environment to verify each step works, (b) test the verification commands return the expected results, (c) confirm the workflow examples (parallel sessions, sandboxed evaluation) actually work, (d) note any gaps surfaced during verification, (e) update the guide accordingly.

Acceptance: a fresh operator following the guide can produce a working sandboxed session with all 14 denyRead paths verified blocked.

title: BOCO telemetry: OTEL Collector host-side sidecar + hook → NR ingest
description:
Closes DEC-007 open follow-up #2 + the Phase 1 BOCO telemetry compliance gap. Architecture per FOLLOWUP.md Q5: PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks emit OTLP to a host-side OTEL Collector on 127.0.0.1:4318; Collector holds NR license key (in /etc/claude-config/credentials/, root-owned, claude-egress-readable via DEC-013) and forwards to otlp.nr-data.net. NR credentials never enter the sandbox or hook env. Hooks run on host so don't need sandbox network allowlist.

Acceptance: a sandboxed session generates OTLP spans visible in BOCO's New Relic account, tagged with _UID=claude-session and session-id. Implementation estimate per FOLLOWUP.md: 6–12 hours. ClaudeConfig-40s.8 (NR ingest key provisioning) is a dependency.

Refs: FOLLOWUP.md Q5 with full pasteable settings.json, Collector YAML, and Python hook skeleton.
notes: DEC-017 (2026-05-27): the PreToolUse/PostToolUse → OTLP hook scripts are language-switched from Python to Lua. The Collector itself is third-party and unaffected. Blocked on ClaudeConfig-58n.1 (Lua provisioning) for the hook handlers.

title: Investigate 2026-05-20 Register disclosure: SOCKS5 hostname null-byte bypass
description:
The Register 2026-05-20 reported a SOCKS5 hostname null-byte injection bypassing /sandbox's wildcard allowlist for ~5.5 months (sandbox GA through v2.1.90). Patched in Claude Code v2.1.90 / sandbox-runtime v0.0.43 / commit fd74a3f. Fix is parser-hardening, NOT structural. Survey already corroborated the patch. This bead is for the deeper followup: inspect the related nine open issues in anthropic-experimental/sandbox-runtime (#97, #122, #131, #149, #160, #193, #225, #273, #274), assess remaining bypass surface, decide whether to upstream improvements vs. rely on DEC-013 egress mediation as sole structural defense.

Refs: docs/research/claude-code-sandboxing-survey.md, .tasks/ClaudeConfig-40s.13-claude-code-sandboxing-survey/FOLLOWUP.md Q4
notes:
DONE 2026-05-26. Findings: .tasks/ClaudeConfig-40s.17-register-disclosure-followup/FINDINGS.md

7 of 9 open issues are STRUCTURAL app-layer gaps (not parser bugs), clustering into the two areas our kernel-layer defenses target:
- Filesystem reads (#122 fail-open, #149 tmpfs-writable, #193 denyRead-inside-allowRead, #160 proc-env-read) → DEC-012 separate-UID + kernel perms defeats all on Linux. Residual gap: #193 means secrets INSIDE an exposed worktree are unprotectable by denyRead — that is the egress broker's job (DEC-013).
- Network egress (#225/#131 allowLocalBinding lifts egress, #274 hostname-only no port-scope) → DEC-013 SNI proxy + nftables (follow-up #2) recovers port-scoping and closes the leak.
- Auto-mode disable (#97 autonomous dangerouslyDisableSandbox) → DEC-013 prohibition + 40s.20 managed-settings ban + DEC-012 floor.
- #273 is a non-security UX feature request.

DECISION: rely on claude-config kernel-layer defenses as the structural backstop; do NOT block on upstream fixes. These nine issues are concrete evidence FOR the augmentation-layer thesis (DEC-011).

Concrete outputs feed: 40s.16 (confirm .env/*_rsa in denyRead + #193 caveat comment), 40s.15 (never emit allowLocalBinding:true or permissive permissionMode), 40s.20 (#97 evidence for managed-settings ban), DEC-013 follow-up #2 nftables (justified by #225/#131/#274).

title: Ship static Claude Code managed-settings (disableAutoMode + permission deny rules)
description:
Re-scoped 2026-05-26 (was: credential deny-list in claude/settings/default.json). The srt-settings credential denyRead baseline is now shipped by the 40s.15.2 emitter; what remains here is the distinct CLAUDE CODE app-layer settings, deployed by the Makefile installer:

1. claude/settings/managed-settings.json → /etc/claude-code/managed-settings.json with permissions.disableAutoMode:"disable" (per 40s.20 — locks off auto mode's .env-credential-egress behavior host-wide). Do NOT force a permissionMode (would break bypassPermissions/dontAsk inside the sandbox).
2. permissions.deny rules in claude/settings/default.json → ~/.claude/settings.json (Read(**/.env) etc. — the 'hard guarantee' per 40s.20; note these are Claude-Code-tool deny rules, separate from srt's filesystem denyRead which the emitter handles).

NB the **/.env glob WORKS for Claude Code's own permission deny rules (app-layer matcher); it does NOT work for srt's Linux filesystem denyRead (bwrap, absolute-only) — see 40s.15.2 finding / DEC-013 correction. These are two different matchers.

Acceptance: installer deploys both; a sandboxed session shows auto mode unavailable and Read(**/.env) denied.
notes:
2026-05-26 (sandbox-wrapper queue): Deferred during autonomous iteration — discovered hard dependency on ClaudeConfig-40s.15. The claude/ source tree, Makefile, and installer do not exist on main yet (subject of 40s.15 / phase1 branch). The deny-list CONTENT is fully specified (see docs/guides/quickstart-sandbox.md, 14 paths) but its landing location and consumption (sandbox.filesystem.denyRead vs permissions.deny, emitter wiring) depend on 40s.15's settings-emitter design. Acceptance criterion (Makefile install + verify in sandboxed session) is unmeetable until 40s.15 ships. Added dep on 40s.15; requeued to tail.

Per 40s.20 findings: the Read(**/.env) deny rule is also the documented "hard guarantee" that neutralizes auto-mode .env-credential behavior — load-bearing beyond just the readability gap.
FINDING (2026-05-26, from 40s.15.2): the 14-path GLOB denyRead baseline this bead was scoped to ship is INEFFECTIVE for srt on Linux. srt's Linux denyRead is bubblewrap-based and matches ABSOLUTE paths / directory subtrees only — filename globs (**/.env, **/*.pem, ~/.aws/**) do NOT match (verified empirically; srt documents globs for macOS only).

Rework for this bead:
- Express the credential deny-list as ABSOLUTE paths, not globs (Linux).
- Recognize the baseline is NARROW defense-in-depth, not the primary defense. Primary read protection in composed mode = DEC-012 UID boundary (claude-session can't read hactar's 0750 home regardless of srt) + DEC-013 broker. 40s.15.2 already ships an absolute, ${SANDBOX_HOME}-anchored narrow baseline in the emitter (claude-session's own .aws/.ssh/.gnupg/.config/gh/.docker/.netrc).
- .env-in-worktree is UNSOLVABLE via denyRead on Linux (can't glob; allowRead dirs can't deny files within them, srt issue #193) → broker only.
- This bead's remaining scope: the static Claude Code settings.json/managed-settings (permissions.deny + disableAutoMode per 40s.20) deployed by the installer, distinct from the srt-settings the emitter produces. Re-evaluate scope vs 40s.15.2 overlap when picked up.

DEC-013 amended with this correction; quickstart srt+/sandbox sections corrected.
DONE 2026-05-27. Shipped claude/settings/managed-settings.json (permissions.disableAutoMode:disable) → /etc/claude-code/managed-settings.json, Makefile-wired (ETC_DIR var, rule, INSTALLED_FILES, install-test override). Validated: jq OK; make install-test deploys to test tree without touching real /etc. SCOPING DECISION: the Read(**/.env)-family permission DENY rules were RE-HOMED to claude-session's own settings (delivered by 40s.15.8 ~/.claude assembly), NOT shipped host-wide here — a host-wide Read(**/.env) deny would block hactar's interactive .env reads too. So this bead = the host-wide disableAutoMode lockoff only; .env deny is claude-session-scoped under 40s.15.8. Also fixed a 40s.15.2 gap: the emitter wasn't in the install map; added EMITTER_SRC/DST + rule so composed mode's wrapper SELF_DIR lookup resolves post-install.

title: Refactor Phase 1 to compose with /sandbox + srt; standalone mode as fallback
description: Implement the augmentation-layer architecture per DEC-011. Wrapper supports two modes: composed (sudo -u claude-session env-scrub srt claude) and --standalone (sudo -u claude-session env-scrub bwrap … -- claude). Both share host-side prep, identity layer (DEC-012), and egress mediation (DEC-013). Profile YAML emits settings.json for composed mode and bwrap args for standalone. Acceptance: both modes smoke-tested per ClaudeConfig-40s.6 expansion. Survey context: .tasks/ClaudeConfig-40s.13-claude-code-sandboxing-survey/
notes:
2026-05-26 (sandbox-wrapper queue): Deferred during autonomous iteration — needs operator input before implementation can start. Three decisions:

1. BRANCH STRATEGY. phase1/wrapper-profile-makefile branch holds the PRE-DEC-011 wrapper (standalone bwrap only; no srt composition; no claude/ settings tree). It has: Makefile, sandbox/bin/claude-sandbox (POC), sandbox/profiles/default.yaml, sandbox/scripts/{provision-claude-session,setup-claude-session-acls}.sh. The DEC-011 refactor changes the wrapper architecture fundamentally (two-mode: composed srt + standalone bwrap). Need a call: build on/reconcile that branch, or branch fresh feat/40s.15-compose-upstream off it per the global accumulator pattern?

2. SYSTEM MUTATION CONSENT. Composed-mode smoke testing (the acceptance criterion) requires 'npm install -g @anthropic-ai/sandbox-runtime' — a global host change I won't make unilaterally.

3. DECOMPOSITION. Bundles wrapper-rewrite + settings.json emitter + standalone bwrap-args builder + two-mode smoke tests. Several modular units; likely warrants child beads (e.g. 40s.15.1 two-mode wrapper skeleton, .2 settings emitter, .3 standalone bwrap args, .4 smoke tests).

Design is already specified (DEC-011/012/013). Implementation inputs from 40s.17 (never emit allowLocalBinding:true; never emit permissive permissionMode) and 40s.20 (emit permissions.disableAutoMode:disable in managed settings) are ready to fold in. Blocks 40s.16.

title: Investigate Claude Code's built-in sandboxing primitives
description:
Surfaced by ClaudeConfig-40s.12. Open question worth resolving early: what does Claude Code itself ship in terms of sandboxing primitives, and how does that overlap with what we're building?

## Why this matters

If Claude Code already isolates tool-use (Bash command execution, file reads/writes, network) at the application layer, some of what claude-config plans to build at the OS layer may be partially redundant, or composable, or — potentially — duplicative. We should understand the upstream surface before continuing to invest in the OS-layer wrapper.

Specifically worth understanding:
- Bash tool: does Claude Code constrain what commands run, with what env? Are deny rules enforced at the Anthropic side or locally?
- File tool: any path restrictions baked in? Or is it purely the local filesystem's view?
- Network: any Claude-Code-side constraints, or all delegated to OS / DNS / firewall?
- The 50-subcommand bypass disclosure (CVE-2026-25723 family) — what's the post-fix model? Is there a structured policy engine in Claude Code now?
- Anthropic's Computer Use reference implementation (Docker-based) — what does it sandbox? Is the design pattern relevant for our Phase 7 dispatcher?

## Acceptance

- Findings written to .tasks/<this-bead-id>-claude-code-sandboxing-survey/REPORT.md
- If material overlap with claude-config's plan is found, file follow-up beads to re-scope
- If gap is identified that Anthropic addresses, note it as a "rely on upstream" decision rather than reimplementing

## Effort estimate

Low — should be 30-60 min of doc skimming and source/release-notes review, NOT a deep code-level investigation. The goal is "do we need to build less because Claude Code already does X?", not a full audit.

title: Escape-hatch: share-credential.sh + optional GPG encryption
description:
Implement the escape-hatch mechanism for sharing user-owned credentials with `claude-session` when a credential cannot be cleanly issued to a separate principal at the upstream service.

## Design (per discussion 2026-05-05)

### Primary mechanism: `share-credential.sh` (option a)

Static copy with optional GPG encryption-at-rest:

```bash
sandbox/scripts/share-credential.sh <hactar-source-path> <claude-session-relative-target> [--encrypt]
```

Steps:
1. Copy <source> to /home/claude-session/<target>
2. chown claude-session:claude-session <target>
3. chmod 600 <target>
4. (optional --encrypt) GPG-encrypt to claude-session's public key; write .gpg file; remove plaintext copy

claude-session's GPG key should be signed by hactar's personal key — the signature IS the audit attestation that hactar deliberately authorized claude-session to hold this credential.

### Escape-hatch-to-the-escape-hatch: option (b)

For credentials that rotate frequently and externally (cloud SSO refresh, pass-managed entries that change behind the scenes), option (b) — bind-mount from hactar's home with ACL — is available as a documented exception. Profile YAML accepts a `shared_credentials.bind_mounts: [...]` field (empty by default; entries trigger setfacl on hactar's source file granting claude-session read access).

## Friction enforcement

Per DEC-008: every tier-1 credential addition requires a DECISION_LOG entry. Shared credentials are tier-1-by-shared-mechanism; the DEC entry must justify why the credential cannot be issued to a separate principal at the upstream service. This is the structural friction.

## Acceptance

- sandbox/scripts/share-credential.sh implemented (option a + optional GPG)
- sandbox/profiles/SCHEMA.md documents the `shared_credentials.bind_mounts:` field (option b escape-hatch-to-the-escape-hatch)
- Documented usage example for the Jira interim case (sibling bead)
- DEC-NNN records the escape-hatch policy formally; cross-referenced from DEC-008
notes:
Filed as DEC-010 (escape-hatch mechanism). The bead's original description said 'DEC-NNN'; that's now resolved to DEC-010.
Re-scoped 2026-05-26 per ClaudeConfig-40s.13 survey + DEC-011/012/013.

This bead remains under the Phase 1 epic but is reframed for the AUGMENTATION-LAYER architecture: claude-config composes with upstream /sandbox + srt (composed mode, default) with a first-class --standalone fallback. Surrounding context shifted; the specific deliverable for this bead may need re-scoping (see survey REPORT.md and FOLLOWUP.md, plus DEC-011 in DECISION_LOG.md). No status change — pick up when ready and re-evaluate scope at that time.

Refs:
- .tasks/ClaudeConfig-40s.13-claude-code-sandboxing-survey/REPORT.md
- .tasks/ClaudeConfig-40s.13-claude-code-sandboxing-survey/FOLLOWUP.md
- docs/research/claude-code-sandboxing-survey.md (condensed)
- DEC-011, DEC-012, DEC-013 in DECISION_LOG.md

title: Supply NR key to host-side OTEL collector; sandbox stays keyless
description: Re-scoped 2026-05-26 (was: Provision claude-session New Relic ingest key (via Erick)). Per DEC-013 + FOLLOWUP Q5, the NR ingest key does NOT enter the sandbox. The OTEL Collector runs host-side and holds the key; the sandbox (and Claude's own OTEL exporter) emit OTLP to localhost:4318 keyless; the collector forwards to otlp.nr-data.net. Key origin: pass professional/Bold_Orange/NewRelic (not the ~/.zshrc plaintext). Preferred: SessionStart hook (runs as hactar) pulls from pass at collector launch — nothing new at rest. Alternative for a standing systemd collector: root-owned /etc/claude-config/credentials/newrelic (0640), populated from pass. Erick confirmed the key has no dangerous permission scopes (low damage radius). Implementation lands with 40s.21 (BOCO telemetry). This bead = make the key available to the collector; it no longer puts anything in claude-session's home.
notes:
Note 2026-05-05: the original description anticipated 'DEC-010 records the credential' — DEC-010 was filed for the escape-hatch policy. The NR credential's DEC entry will be the next available number when the credential is actually provisioned (likely DEC-011).
Re-scoped 2026-05-26 per ClaudeConfig-40s.13 survey + DEC-011/012/013.

This bead remains under the Phase 1 epic but is reframed for the AUGMENTATION-LAYER architecture: claude-config composes with upstream /sandbox + srt (composed mode, default) with a first-class --standalone fallback. Surrounding context shifted; the specific deliverable for this bead may need re-scoping (see survey REPORT.md and FOLLOWUP.md, plus DEC-011 in DECISION_LOG.md). No status change — pick up when ready and re-evaluate scope at that time.

Refs:
- .tasks/ClaudeConfig-40s.13-claude-code-sandboxing-survey/REPORT.md
- .tasks/ClaudeConfig-40s.13-claude-code-sandboxing-survey/FOLLOWUP.md
- docs/research/claude-code-sandboxing-survey.md (condensed)
- DEC-011, DEC-012, DEC-013 in DECISION_LOG.md
RECOMMENDATION (2026-05-26): do NOT expose the NR key to claude-session / the sandbox. Per DEC-013 + FOLLOWUP Q5, the OTEL Collector runs HOST-SIDE and holds the key; the sandbox (and Claude's own OTEL exporter) emit OTLP to localhost:4318 KEYLESS; the collector forwards to otlp.nr-data.net. Key never enters the sandbox → no in-sandbox exfil surface even under a bypass.

Key origin = pass professional/Bold_Orange/NewRelic (drop the ~/.zshrc plaintext as source of truth). Two collector-launch models:
- SessionStart hook (runs as hactar): pull from pass at launch — cleanest, nothing new at rest, key stays in hactar's control. RECOMMENDED.
- standing systemd service: root-owned /etc/claude-config/credentials/newrelic (0640, collector-UID-readable, per DEC-013), populated from pass.

This sidesteps DEC-008's tier-1 model (the key is host telemetry infra, not a sandbox credential) and avoids needing a per-credential tier-1 DEC entry. The DEC-010 GPG-signed escape hatch (chown+chmod600+GPG, signature = authorization record) remains the right pattern for a credential that must live INSIDE the sandbox — the NR key isn't one.

User confirmed with Erick the NR ingest key has no dangerous permission scopes (low damage radius). Re-scope candidate: 40s.8 → "supply NR key to host-side OTEL collector; sandbox stays keyless." Implementation lands with 40s.21.

title: Jira/Atlassian readonly token (claude-session, interim personal token)
description:
Create a personal Jira/Atlassian API token with a Claude-adjacent name (e.g., "claude-session-readonly") for auditability, and transfer ownership to `claude-session` via the escape-hatch mechanism. This is the *interim* tier-1 Jira credential pending a dedicated service account from BOCO IT (see sibling bead).

## Mechanism

Per the escape-hatch (DEC-NNN, pending): user creates token at id.atlassian.com with a clearly Claude-attributed name. Transfer to claude-session via `share-credential.sh` (option a — copy + chown + chmod 600, optionally GPG-encrypted to claude-session's pubkey).

## Scope discipline

The token inherits the user's Atlassian permissions, which is broader than ideal. Mitigation: rely on the Claude-attributed name for audit trails, document what scopes/projects Claude actually uses (BOCO project keys), and prioritize the service-account follow-up.

## Acceptance

- Token created in id.atlassian.com with a name that makes attribution obvious in Jira audit logs (e.g., "claude-session-readonly-<date>")
- Token transferred to /home/claude-session/.config/atlassian/token via share-credential.sh
- A sandboxed Claude session can read Jira tickets via the Atlassian MCP tool (or jira-cli equivalent)
- DEC-011 records the credential as a tier-1-by-escape-hatch entry per DEC-008

## Supersession

Closes when the dedicated service account (sibling bead) is provisioned.
notes: Note 2026-05-05: the original description anticipated 'DEC-011 records the credential' — that number was reserved for escape-hatch implications and may shift. The interim Jira credential's DEC entry will be the next available number when provisioned (likely DEC-012, given DEC-011 may go to NR).

title: bd-timew init-project: doesn't scaffold .beads/bd-timew.yaml sidecar
description:
`bd-timew init-project --server` registers the repo with the global cleanup config (~/.config/bd-timew/repos.yaml) but does NOT create the per-project sidecar at `.beads/bd-timew.yaml`. The user has to discover and run `bd-timew config init` separately to scaffold the sidecar.

Result: time tracking against beads in the new project produces `Client/Case/Svc: (none)` until the user notices the gap and runs `config init` manually.

Discovered 2026-05-05 starting work on ClaudeConfig-40s.4 in claude-config — the original migration ran `init-project` per the published recipe, the sidecar was never created, and the gap surfaced only when the first `bd-timew start` showed empty tuple values.

Proposed fix: `bd-timew init-project` should run `bd-timew config init` as part of project bootstrap (or warn loudly when no sidecar exists at the end of init-project). Either approach makes new-project setup correct-by-default.

Related (init-project gap family):
- J121-i85 (auto-push hang during settings bootstrap)
- J121-xji (bootstrap bd init when .beads/ is missing)

title: Reviewer dispatch
description: Coordinator spawns a claude-sandbox read-only checkout of a worker's branch. Reviewer examines git diff main..., posts review via bead comment. No filesystem access to the worker's working directory; collaboration happens through git only.

title: Bead state machine + heartbeat/timeout pattern
description: State machine: open → claimed → in_progress → ready_for_review → reviewed → ready_to_merge → merged. Heartbeat from worker prevents orphaned in_progress beads. Timeout escalation surfaces stuck workers.

title: Coordinator/human merge process documentation
description: Document the bd merge workflow: how the coordinator (or human) reviews an agent's claude/* branch and merges to main. Include the non-fast-forward case where main has moved during the session.

title: Coordinator process design + skeleton
description: Long-lived systemd-managed coordinator. Reads bd ready --label=area:agent-tasks, dispatches workers, observes their progress, dispatches reviewers, surfaces ready-to-merge work. Single-file design doc + initial skeleton.

title: Worker dispatch
description: Coordinator spawns a claude-sandbox session in a fresh worktree + Dolt branch, scoped to one bead. Profile selection per task class. Worker runs, commits, pushes branch, updates bead status.

title: Branch-pattern GRANTs for bd-claude-session
description: GRANT SELECT on all branches; GRANT INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE only on branches matching claude/*. Verify with negative test: an attempt to write to main fails with auth denied.

title: Per-session claude/<session-id> Dolt branch lifecycle
description: Wrapper creates a Dolt branch matching the git session-id at sandbox startup; deletes (or merges) at session end. BEADS_DOLT_USER and BEADS_DOLT_PASSWORD for bd-claude-session set inside the namespace; user's own bd-write credentials not exposed.

title: bd-claude-session Dolt user creation script
description: SQL bootstrap script that creates the bd-claude-session Dolt user with a generated password (stored in pass). Idempotent: re-runs do not error if the user exists.

title: memory/agent-observations/ subdirectory convention
description: Auto-memory MEMORY.md does not auto-load this subtree. Agent observations land here; human reviews and promotes selected entries to the main memory tree. Treat agent memory like agent code — propose, review, promote.

title: Verify Phase 1 ACLs grant write-back through bind mount
description: Verify that the ACLs set up in Phase 1 grant claude-session write access to ~/.claude/projects/<hash>/ when accessed through the namespace bind mount. bd memories writes from inside the sandbox must reach the canonical location.

title: Concurrent-session smoke test
description: Verify two parallel claude-sandbox invocations are independent: each in its own worktree, neither sees the other's filesystem; pushed branches visible only via git fetch. Documented procedure in docs/SANDBOX_GUIDE.md.

title: Bind-mount worktree at canonical path inside namespace
description: Inside the agent's mount namespace, bind-mount the worktree at the canonical project path (/home/hactar/Source/<project>/). Same path string the user's interactive session uses, so the project-hash for ~/.claude/projects/<hash>/ matches. See Common Pitfall #2.

title: Reference profiles: default + full-trust
description: Ship reference profiles in this repo: default.yaml (baseline, nothing extra exposed) and full-trust.yaml (escape hatch for emergency parity with pre-sandbox behavior). Documentation for when to use full-trust.

title: Session ID generation + tracking
description: Session IDs (timestamp + short random suffix). Wrapper records active sessions in a state file or via systemd-run scope. Used by Phase 5 for claude/<session-id> Dolt branches.

title: Auto-worktree creation + teardown
description: git worktree add -b session-<id> <path> origin/main at session start; git worktree remove at session end. Idempotent.

title: Profile YAML schema design + documentation
description: Document the profile schema in sandbox/profiles/SCHEMA.md. Keys: visible filesystem paths (read-only / read-write), worktree policy (auto-create / use-existing / disabled), skill subset, network policy. Schema enforcement: profiles MUST NOT contain credentials: or env: keys for secret injection (DEC-001). Loader rejects offending profiles.

title: Profile loader implementation
description: Wrapper reads ~/.config/claude-sandbox/profiles/<active>.yaml at session start; consumed before claude exec. Schema validation at load time.

title: Profile discovery: --profile flag + per-project .claude/sandbox-profile
description: Two discovery mechanisms: (a) --profile <name> on the wrapper, (b) per-project .claude/sandbox-profile file naming the active profile. Default to 'default' if neither present.

title: Phase 1 manual smoke test documentation
description: Document the manual smoke test (claude-sandbox --version; claude-sandbox -p 'echo hello'; verify credentials empty; verify ~/.claude bind-mount). Lives in docs/SANDBOX_GUIDE.md. Manual rather than CI-driven because CI cannot easily execute the privileged namespace setup.

No status change — pick up when ready and re-evaluate scope at that time.

- docs/research/claude-code-sandboxing-survey.md (condensed)
- DEC-011, DEC-012, DEC-013 in DECISION_LOG.md

title: One-time OAuth flow for claude-session
description: claude-session does its own one-time browser OAuth flow with the user's company subscription. Credentials stored in /home/claude-session/.claude/.credentials.json. Document the bootstrap experience: first claude-sandbox invocation lands in a session with no auth; the user completes the browser flow inside that sandboxed session.

Re-scoped 2026-05-26 per ClaudeConfig-40s.13 survey + DEC-011/012/013.

This bead remains under the Phase 1 epic but is reframed for the AUGMENTATION-LAYER architecture: claude-config composes with upstream /sandbox + srt (composed mode, default) with a first-class --standalone fallback.
